programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The team is called to the Miami Grand Prix, where a pit crew member has been killed in a mysterious fire while refuelling his driver's car. At first the death appears to be accidental - but as events unfold, Horatio uncovers evidence pointing to murder. David Caruso, Emily Procter and Adam Rodriguez star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tries to locate a kidnapped girl but has to contend with her grandfather, a former member of a disbanded special forces unit. The agents discover he has been secretly negotiating with the kidnapper, who was once in his undercover team and has decided to kill off his old colleagues in a bid to atone for their actions. Meanwhile, Abby is forced to work with an intern, and makes him follow her idiosyncratic rules. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Three men stage a raid on a drug deal, taking a suitcase full of cash and killing the dealer and an undercover LAPD officer. While studying footage taken from CCTV equipment in the house, Sam recognises the distinctive hand signals of one of the suspects as being those of Navy Seal Kurt Holgate, who he served with in Bosnia. Eric studies the details of his unit and finds that a man called Jackson has been unaccounted for since the day of the attack. Drama, starring LL Cool J

programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: David throws a party for Bianca's 18th birthday, Nigel lays a ghost to rest and Pat wonders what is going on between David and Cindy

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Jack becomes impatient for a result in the undercover operation to trap drug dealer Jose Alvarez, and Zain panics when he's pressed to set up a sting which would bring Kristen Shaw down as well. Roger and Dan deal with a troublesome family who intend to spend their lottery win on one big party and Emma is left holding the baby, while James Tennant prepares for his court appearance by getting blind drunk

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Joseph finds himself stuck in a lift with Daisha as she goes into labour, and delivers a boy - but the happiness of the occasion is marred by Faye's response. Meanwhile, Linden feels aggrieved at having a proposal turned down by the board, and Michael tries to avoid working alongside wife Annalese - until a crisis leaves him with no choice

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A deaf girl accidentally runs over her father, Harry is furious when Simon and Tally arrive for work having obviously spent the night together, and Fin is angry with Comfort for putting herself in danger on her first day back. Mike fails to ease Tess's concerns about his recent resignation, and Roxy lets slip to Bex that she is pregnant

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: With the Serious Organised Crime Agency muscling in on his case, Jack orders Zain to take a break and brings Kristen Shaw in for questioning, but he's forced to let her go when she refuses to name the big boys in the Colombian drugs operation. Dan is sent home when Gina learns his steroid use caused him to lose control in the boxing match, leaving his opponent struggling for life. Lottery winners the Hendersons provide further proof money can't buy happiness

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Bianca's birthday party becomes a bone of contention. Grant decides to make up for some lost time, and Cindy puts an ultimatum to David

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: David throws a party for Bianca's 18th birthday, Nigel lays a ghost to rest and Pat wonders what is going on between David and Cindy

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Anna and Gino's romance continues to blossom as they sneak off to spend time together - even though it's clear that something about their secret trysts is playing on Gino's mind. Meanwhile, at Ash Park, Olivia and James's relationship is at breaking point as Olivia struggles with the identity of James's lover. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Joanna discovers a patient's seemingly trivial snoring problem hides a deep-seated problem requiring sensitive handling. Andrew grabs every opportunity to continue his affair with Kate, and the unexpected reappearance of a childhood sweetheart forces Sam to confront his feelings for Kerri. Gary Mavers, Haydn Gwynne and John McCardle star

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James encounters a savage pig, but finds it a much less daunting challenge than making a speech at the youth club. Rural drama, staring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: Hester wins a leg of mutton at the village fete, but the prize turns out to be four legs - still attached to the sheep. Comedy, starring Julia McKenzie and Anton Rodgers

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom and Diana take a conservationist stance when faced with Harvey's plans to extend Bayview, and Geoffrey hits it off with Jane. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: The end of the war sees Rene, Edith and the rest of the gang welcoming in the Allied troops, and they soon find that the invasion party includes a couple of old friends

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg and Truly fail to bring a smile to the face of Billy's humourless brother-in-law Bradley, until Auntie Wainwright passes by with an inflatable animal feeding trough. Peter Sallis stars

programme.title: Kavanagh QC
programme.desc: A policewoman is murdered, and when a blood-soaked shoe is found in a suspect's wardrobe, it seems to be an open-and-shut case - but Kavanagh is not convinced the right man is in the dock. John Thaw stars

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team looks into the death of a terrorist 30 years previously after his daughter receives an anonymous note claiming he was murdered - an investigation that takes them right back to the Greenham Common anti-nuclear protests. Meanwhile, Sasha agrees to go for dinner with her ex-husband Ned, which he is hoping will lead to a reconciliation. Tamzin Outhwaite and Dennis Waterman star, with guest appearances from Barnaby Kay, Charlotte Cornwell and Patricia Potter

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The team's attempt to prevent the car bomb plot to assassinate the Home Secretary has serious consequences for Juliet. The conspirators place pressure on the weakened Prime Minister, forcing him to pass measures that will turn Britain into a dictatorship. Harry races to stop them, but is taken prisoner by treacherous MI6 boss Collingwood - who plans to have him killed

programme.title: The Bill

Dan is sent home when Gina learns his steroid use caused him to lose control in the boxing match, leaving his opponent struggling for life. Lottery winners the Hendersons provide further proof money can't buy happiness

programme.desc: Period medical drama chronicling the personal and professional consequences of Eleanor Bramwell's determined quest to become the first female surgeon at the East London Hospital. Jemma Redgrave stars

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Nikki is flattered by the persistent advances of a man who has brought his best friend into the department for treatment, until shocking revelations emerge about Jim's past, prompting her to finish with him. Keith's plan to get Claire alone almost leads to a fatal accident when he is hit by a car after chasing her down a causeway, and Tally struggles to cope with Harry's disapproval of Simon

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Stuart and Terry are surprised to discover ex-colleague DC Jo Masters working undercover when they investigate a counterfeit satellite navigation systems operation, and learn that they have stumbled upon a much bigger crime involving ruthless gun-runners. Honey and Leela sort out a scuffle at a funeral involving feuding brothers and end up falling for the same smooth-talking Irishman

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah heads to Sydney with George and Anna to see her ill mother, while Elizabeth decides to reveal her darkest secret in the hope that it will save James and Olivia's marriage. Elsewhere, Sir Henry Swanson makes a request to receive a Japanese trade delegation at Ash Park

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Sam's medical bag is stolen, leading to a crisis for one of his patients. An accident at a local knitwear factory presents Andrew with a case of divided loyalties, while Joanna and Tom face tough decisions about their future. Rural medical drama, starring Gary Mavers and Joseph Millson, with guest appearances by Keith Barron and Carol Drinkwater

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James saves the life of a calf, but finds the difficult delivery easier than accepting the farmer's gratitude. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison, and featuring an appearance by 'Allo 'Allo's Gorden Kaye

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: Hester and Emma disagree with William over the family holiday. He wishes to go motoring through France, but they have something more nautical in mind. Comedy, starring Julia McKenzie and Anton Rodgers

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Bayview prepares for a royal visit, and while Tom is thrilled at the prospect, Diana struggles to contain her obvious displeasure. Meanwhile, slow-on-the-uptake Jane starts to suspect Harvey is being unfaithful and wonders whether to look for someone new. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Daddy has an accident while chasing a milkwoman on his bicycle, and ends up heavily sedated in hospital. First-ever episode of the suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and George Webb

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg, Truly and Billy offer words of wisdom to a broken-hearted man they find seeking solace in the woods. Peter Sallis and Hywel Bennett star

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: Two bodies are discovered on a life raft in Ystad harbour, the victims having been shot through the head and had their tongues burnt off with acid. The heavily tattooed corpses reveal links to the Russian mob and are instrumental in proving the men originated from Latvia, so Major Karlis Liepa, an officer with the country's police department, is sent to work with Wallander. But an anonymous phone call, a shooting and a break-in at the police HQ soon convince Wallander of a leak in the investigation - and he immediately suspects Liepa. Guest starring Soren Malling (The Killing), with Kenneth Branagh as the world-weary detective

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A 79-year-old woman with dementia runs away from her care home and turns up at a south London police station to report a murder - sparking a reinvestigation into the mysterious disappearance of her police officer husband in 1956. The official report says that he fell into the river after one too many beers - but what secrets was this seemingly straight-as-a die old-fashioned bobby keeping? Meanwhile, Steve's son is arrested for buying weed, bringing the retired cop face to face with his ex-wife - a woman he hasn't seen in 10 years. Tamzin Outhwaite and Denis Lawson star, with a guest appearance from Julie Graham

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Zaf risks his life on an undercover mission to infiltrate an al-Qaeda cell planning a bomb attack on the UK, in an attempt to urge a member of the organisation to switch sides. Meanwhile, Adam learns the terrorists are embarking upon a suicide mission that will put his colleagues in great danger, and attempts to reach their target as events spiral out of control. Raza Jaffrey and Rupert Penry-Jones star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Stuart and Terry are surprised to discover ex-colleague DC Jo Masters working undercover when they investigate a counterfeit satellite navigation systems operation, and learn that they have stumbled upon a much bigger crime involving ruthless gun-runners. Honey and Leela sort out a scuffle at a funeral involving feuding brothers and end up falling for the same smooth-talking Irishman

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Wealthy widow Alice Costigan invites Robert to stay at her country house for a spot of pheasant shooting, filling him with high hopes of romance. However, he's not the only one vying for her affections, and when her nephew falls ill she snubs him by turning to his rival for help. Victorian medical drama, starring Jemma Redgrave, David Calder, Maureen Beattie and Andrew Connolly

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Marsham learns his wife has breast cancer and tries to make amends for neglecting her, shelving a planned move to Scotland and begging Robert and Eleanor to perform a life-saving operation. Medical drama, starring Jemma Redgrave and David Calder

programme.desc: Faye's plea for space leads to a recurrence of Joseph's obsessive behaviour and he turns to drink. Donna lets Maria down, and Max is offered a place at a football academy in Holland, forcing Kyla to rethink her priorities. Meanwhile, Sam is worried by a series of strange phone calls and apparent wrong numbers

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Comfort is disgusted when female members of staff strike raunchy poses for a charity calendar, especially when it's revealed that a woman benefiting from the money is only after a free holiday. Tally informs Simon that Harry has been self-prescribing, Claire gets the blame when Keith takes an overdose, and Nikki finds it hard to refuse Jim's offer to match the cost of the plane fare to New York

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Reg and Tony are called to assist a council bailiff trying to evict a pregnant woman from her flat, but end up arresting the official for violent conduct. Jo's undercover operation to snare the gun-runners takes a sinister turn when supplier Philip Hanson is found dead in the boot of his car. Leela admits to Honey she took Irish charmer Brad Durkin home the previous night

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: David gets a shock when he boards the plane to Spain, Bianca gets fed up with Ricky, and Steve gets more than he bargained for when he decides to try his luck

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ricky and Bianca prove just how stubborn they can be, whilst Grant puts Phil's loyalty to the test. David embarks on a holiday romance

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Elizabeth is rushed to hospital after suffering a heart attack, and is looked after tenderly by Jack, but comes close to revealing a secret from her past while heavily sedated. Her frail health makes Anna and Gino decide to postpone telling their families about their relationship, but Carla becomes suspicious after finding contraception hidden in Anna's room

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: A bitter feud between a local farmer and a fairground family explodes into violence, with tragic consequences for pregnant Joanna, while Andrew dices with danger by continuing his illicit affair with Kate. Medical drama, starring Gary Mavers, Haydn Gwynne and Joseph Millson

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Helen returns after attending her aunt's funeral, but James has a number of calls on his time, including dealing with complications arising during the birth of a calf. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: Hester and William's lease on their much-loved farmhouse comes to an end, and their obnoxious English neighbours try to purchase it. Julia McKenzie and Anton Rodgers star

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Geoffrey persuades Diana to see a doctor about her foot, but his suggestion of adopting a new diet doesn't go down well

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth invites the new vicar round for tea - but despite her meticulous planning, the afternoon does not go as planned. Sitcom about a super-snob intent on climbing the social ladder, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Jeremy Gittins

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: A hang-gliding enthusiast crashes down in the village, mistakenly believing that he's landed in France, thus breaking the long-distance record. The gang tries to compensate for his disappointment by making him the best at something else - identifying a strange cat creature lurking in the woods. Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Brian Murphy star

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The locals are unhappy when an ostentatious American opens a golf club near Kembleford - only for a body to turn up in the reeds. Whodunit, with Mark Williams

programme.title: Frankie Drake Mysteries
programme.desc: Frankie steps into the boxing ring and meets up with her old friend Moses Page as part of a plan to stop illegal boxing and take down a corrupt cop. Period crime drama, starring Lauren Lee Smith as the first female private detective in 1920s Toronto

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sasha is forced to work with her ex-husband Ned Hancock when their teams investigate the death of film critic Oliver Houghton, whose body was discovered floating in the Thames. UCOS delves into the victim's past and explores his link to the murder of a conceptual artist 20 years previously, while Hancock's knowledge of London's hidden past proves invaluable when a piece of occult footage from the 1970s plunges the investigators into the macabre world of human sacrifice. Tamzin Outhwaite and Denis Lawson star, with a guest appearance by Barnaby Kay

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Team members are posted undercover at the heart of the talks during the World Trade Summit for Africa, where they unearth an assassination plot which they must risk their lives to quash. However, they are faced with a moral dilemma when they realise the true motivations behind the deadly plan. High-octane drama, starring Rupert Penry-Jones, with Alex Jennings

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Reg and Tony are called to assist a council bailiff trying to evict a pregnant woman from her flat, but end up arresting the official for violent conduct. Jo's undercover operation to snare the gun-runners takes a sinister turn when supplier Philip Hanson is found dead in the boot of his car. Leela admits to Honey she took Irish charmer Brad Durkin home the previous night

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor is flattered by the attentions of charming Dr Hunter, at least on first impressions, but soon changes her opinion when his housekeeper dies suddenly

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor's absence is sorely felt, leaving Robert to take on a young woman who hopes to be a medical student, although Dr Marsham is far from impressed

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Petting Arlo, doing his blocks and even drinking juice hurts after Bing breaks his arm. He tries drawing with Sula and when it doesn't hurt, forgets his arm and does a big, painful hoppity voosh. Amma gently explains that it's okay to ask for help

programme.title: Halibalw
programme.desc: Children's entertainment, with singing and dancing

programme.title: Octonots

programme.desc: Jay Blades and a team of experts transform a pair of chairs, a collection of metal racks and drawers, and a school desk at Walsall Recycling Centre

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are offered to chance to bid on a vintage table hockey game, an early-20th-century glass chandelier and antique sketch books that belonged to the artist Henry Thomson

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio, presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: The Great Staycation
programme.desc: Mathew Baynton narrates a documentary following the fortunes of a luxury boutique hotel in the Essex town as owners Clare and Javed try to pull off a successful bank holiday. Fully booked, short on staff and with a heatwave looming, tensions are rising in this family-run operation. But will the visitors love it or loathe it?

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Ceramics conservator Kirsten Ramsay is charged with the task of repairing a little cat teapot with an extraordinary wartime story behind it, while Dominic Chinea takes on the restoration of a once brightly coloured wooden carousel horse. The team also brings a wooden boat puzzle and a Victorian magic lantern back to life

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Crewmates Adam and Dan treat a patient who has fallen down a flight of stairs in the casino, while Rachel and Stuart respond to a call when a child falls from a bunk bed. As the shift continues, a distressing call from a patient threatening to jump into the river takes priority, and the call handler must try to keep the patient talking until help can arrive

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: Six unsigned MCs compete to become the next rap superstar, as they go head-to-head in a battle to sign for rap legends Krept and Konan's record label. In the first episode of the new series, the artists perform for the first time in front of rap legends Krept, Konan, and DJ Target, demonstrating their A Capella skills, before rapping about a key moment in their life to guest mentor Rapman

programme.title: Rap Trip: Underground Scenes Uncovered
programme.desc: Rappers FOS and Ransom travel to Liverpool to learn about Scouse Rap. They meet a 16-year-old female drill artist and a rapper from the Wirral who raps about his autism

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host this weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a two-bedroom maisonette in London, a house in Barrow-in-Furness, Cumbria, and a mid-terrace in Goldenhill, Stoke-on-Trent

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: A tenant making more than £100,000 unlawfully subletting his council flat, and a criminal gang running a multimillion-pound diesel fraud

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A truck driver is diverted along a narrow road that cannot cope with the weight of his vehicle, with disastrous consequences. Paramedics treat an unresponsive baby

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Presenter Anita Manning leads proceedings at the Yorkshire Events Centre in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, where Kate Bliss and Mark Stacey help two teams to find bargains to take to auction for a profit. Anita also meets up with a plucky pal with an interesting musical collection from a legendary entertainer

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Consumers test and score an eco-lunchbox, an all-in-one waterproof coat for children and a cafetiere accessory that avoids having to scoop up the mushy grains when finished

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple from Sunderland with £210,000 navigate the fast-paced property market of North Yorkshire, focusing on the popular towns of Easingwold, Thirsk and Malton

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours the skips at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey for things to restore and sell for a profit. Items include a pair of leather wallets and a rocking chair

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including an unusual musical instrument, a wartime phone being sold for charity, and a cake stand with cakes thrown in. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Honey's date takes a terrifying turn when unbeknown to her, Paul spikes her drink. Ruby tells Kush to return the money he stole and she will forget it ever happened - but he denies it, leaving her furious and determined to get revenge on the Slaters. Shirley tries to talk to Mick, but he pushes her too far by saying she was never there for him

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg and Chris are in Bromley with the Winbourne family, who desperately want to change their ways. Busy mum Clare and daughters Rachel and Sadie all rely on dad James to cook the family meals, but not everyone is satisfied with what's being rustled up. With the same meals on rotation and a lack of fruit and veg being consumed, the whole family are bored when it comes to mealtimes, and often resort to eating out

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at the interview stage and a helpful guide on how to survive the process, which sees the last few candidates go toe-to-toe with Alan Sugar's trusted business associates - Claude Littner, Linda Plant and Mike Soutar - as their CVs and business plans are heavily scrutinised

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the audience. Presented by Fiona Bruce

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Another chance to this intimate portrait of an exceptionally gifted musical family in lockdown, the Kanneh-Masons. Ever since lockdown began, the seven young prodigies aged 10 to 24 have been isolated in the family home in Nottingham, along with their parents Stuart and Kadiatu. Unable to perform publicly, the family decided to stage a vibrant and eclectic concert in the only place they can - their own home - and granted the BBC exclusive access using fixed rig cameras installed in the house that were remotely operated

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the development progress of properties in Pimlico, London, Ilkeston, Derbyshire and Chesterton, near Newcastle-under-Lyme

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: In Birmingham, the investigators crack down on the callous travel agent ripping off British holidaymakers, taking their money but never booking their flights. In light of the story of one limo driving a school party of 11-year-olds bursting into flames, there's also some spot-checking on luxury limos being driven in London

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A couple whose car was hit by a truck on the M1 while on a journey to celebrate their wedding anniversary. Plus, a wing suit free-fall enthusiast who hit trouble when his main parachute became tangled up, 30 seconds from the ground. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In a rock and pop special, Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to the iconic BBC Maida Vale music studios, and gets the lowdown on an incredible collection of music memorabilia

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers unbox, test and score a lint roller that specialises in pet hair, a storage solution for tin cans, and a plastic stick that can be shaped to help with various DIY tasks

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Sonali Shah makes a beeline for the desirable town of Sevenoaks in Kent, to help buyers who are selling up their London flat and sinking their £900,000 budget into a new life

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore searches for tired and unwanted items at Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey she can transform from skip-bound to stylish. With three items saved, Sarah turns to upholsterer Sharon O'Connor to overhaul an old chair frame, while upcycler Jacqui Joseph attempts to transform a dated desk. Sarah's challenge is to update a set of four chairs. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers and valuation expert Simon Bower welcome four more people with interesting items they'd like to sell. Nigel then invites the dealers to bid against one another to secure a vintage fairground game, a 19th-century Chinese inkwell, a 1950s toy pedal car and a Victorian watchman's clock

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances. Presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker hosts the light-hearted quiz, with Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell heading teams of celebrities in a test of their sporting knowledge. Taking part this time around are horse racing legend AP McCoy, World Cup-winning cricketer Charlotte Edwards, former England goalkeeper David James and Olympic cycling champion Joanna Rowsell Shand

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedian and writer David Baddiel, former MP and Strictly Come Dancing 2016 star Ed Balls, stand-up and actress Jo Brand and ex-Pussycat Doll and Celebrity MasterChef 2015 winner Kimberly Wyatt join host Rob Brydon and captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Suki proposes a poker game with Vinny and Kheerat at Ruby's club and suggests they invite Kush, but he is suspicious of her motive and declines. However, when he later turns up he finds them ready to play - and cannot resist joining in. Meanwhile, Shirley offers Gray a job in the Mitchell family business

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Channel 4 presenter Steph McGovern hosts the satirical quiz, inviting comedian Miles Jupp and Baroness Sayeeda Warsi to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: With Dave and Denise's wedding fast approaching, the arrival of a huge phone bill is the last thing Jim needs. Another chance to see the first-ever episode of the award-winning comedy, starring Caroline Aherne, Craig Cash, Ricky Tomlinson and Sue Johnston

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham is joined by Irish actor and singer Jessie Buckley, starring in Charlie Kaufman's Netflix drama I'm Thinking of Ending Things. Also appearing are comedian Bill Bailey - one of this year's Strictly contestants - plus fellow comic and children's author David Walliams. Singer Dermot Kennedy performs his single Giants, and the host offers a wry look at some trending news stories

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Edmund and Nora prepare to launch their app, and Wally's dinner date goes terribly awry

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: After Scrubr gets acquired by a company overseas, Nora moves to China. Back in Queens, Grandma befriends an injured pigeon

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at the interview stage and a helpful guide on how to survive the process, which sees the last few candidates go toe-to-toe with Alan Sugar's trusted business associates - Claude Littner, Linda Plant and Mike Soutar - as their CVs and business plans are heavily scrutinised

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Investigating cases of coach drivers who continue to drive while exhausted. Plus, new imaging technology used to discover if sheds are being illegally rented out as homes

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades enlists woodworking expert Ollie Allen to give a coffee table a makeover, while upcycler Jacqui Joseph breathes new life into a set of dining chairs

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including St Helens v Salford Red Devils, Leeds Rhinos v Castleford Tigers and Wakefield Trinity v Hull Kingston Rovers

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: The genealogy documentary follows more celebrities as they trace their family trees to uncover facts about their heritage. First up is Doctor Who star Jodie Whittaker, who looks into a romantic story she has been told about how her paternal grandmother Greta came to be given the middle name of Verdun - which was a First World War battle. On her mother's side, Jodie gets to the bottom of how her great-great-grandfather worked his way up from child labourer in a Yorkshire coal mine to becoming the owner of one

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: Quiz show in which a team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Philip Serrell, Tony Wong and Micaela Sharp search Stratford-upon-Avon for items, choosing a bottle-corking machine, some lamps and old furniture in the hope of turning a profit

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Arlo is desperate to go into catering and gives up his major school subjects, much to the concern of George. Woody thinks Tracey is taking on too much with her new job

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The ten remaining home sewers return to the nation's most famous sewing room for children's week, and are asked to make a classic smocked dress in the pattern challenge. The task requires the sewers to combine tricky techniques, including making their own bias binding, and a Peter Pan collar. Next, in the transformation challenge, the sewers let their imaginations run wild, turning sleeping bags into food shaped fancy dress. Finally, the contestants are asked to make a pair of dungarees for a little boy or girl

programme.title: Italy's Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Using the latest 3D scanning technology, Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott uncover the hidden history of three Italian cities, beginning with Naples, from its earliest Greek and Roman origins. The pair explore the impact of the volcano of Mount Vesuvius, and how it both nurtured the region and exacted a terrible price on the local population. Alexander and Michael also delve into a labyrinth of underground spaces constructed by ruling Bourbon kings in the 19th century, that helped to sustain the city

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A special episode revisiting some of the historic venues visited during this series, including Bamburgh Castle in Northumberland

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jayde Adams, Josie d'Arby, Rufus Hound and Scott Mills go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball gives all the backstage gossip and exclusive news from Strictly Come Dancing, and Ian Waite gives his thoughts on the couples' training progress.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Peter Andre and Joe Pasquale travel around Kent in search of antiques, assisted by experts Paul Laidlaw and Catherine Southon. Joe persuades expert Catherine Southon to buy a pommel horse, while Paul Laidlaw impresses his celebrity sidekick Peter with a pressed poppy from the battlefields of the First World War

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams all look at the unseen side of autumn from their four separate locations across the country. Tonight, Michaela shares more drama from the Scottish seal colony, and Chris reveals the latest antics of the multi-coloured badger family

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Charlotte is determined to get her own back on Isaac Pincher who has burned down her house. Lucy suggests a novel way of drumming up business for her new enterprise with Elizabeth and Fredo Harvey. Meanwhile, Lydia makes a new friend in Bedlam

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits the historic city of Cadiz in southern Spain, where he enjoys traditional tapas dishes including chickpea stews, cured pork lardons and freshly grilled mackerel

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: A light-hearted look at the social and cultural changes society went through since the turn of the 21st century, featuring contributions from politicians and celebrities. In the second episode, Angela Scanlon, Amol Rajan and Emma Barnett take a light-hearted look back at the year 2001, from the first Harry Potter film to hit cinemas, to a gripping EastEnders storyline

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The Dark Shadow of Murder
programme.desc: The mother of murder victim Moira Jones shares her personal account of learning to cope with grief and using her experience to help others coping with similar tragedies

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The Fraserburgh crew race to save a stricken fishing boat without a working rudder in a force-eight gale. On Loch Ness, a yacht becomes trapped on a weir, and one wrong move by the lifeboat crew could send it over the edge. On the north Devon coast, the Minehead crew comes to the aid of a puppy that has swallowed a fishing hook

programme.title: The Shipman Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: The final edition opens by looking at the first report of the public inquiry into the deaths of Harold Shipman's patients, concluding that while working as a GP he had murdered 260 of them. Chris Wilson explores the reaction to the staggering total, exploring how attention began to focus on the killer as experts and commentators came up with a range of psychological profiles and theories to try to understand why one, seemingly ordinary doctor had killed so many people

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness each buy a sporty insurance write-off for £6,000 and take part in three challenges at the programme's test track before they're ordered to make their cars road legal and report to Alexandra Palace, where the producers have constructed a monstrous Wall of Death. The trio also celebrate the era of the 200mph supercar by going on a road trip in a Ferrari F40, a Lamborghini Diablo, and a Jaguar XJ220

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers hunt for counterfeit and dangerous goods at Suffolk's Felixstowe Port. Elsewhere, a gang who callously dumped tons of illegal waste are tracked down

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades and a team of experts transform a pair of chairs, a collection of metal racks and drawers, and a school desk at Walsall Recycling Centre

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: The High Court enforcement officers face an angry car dealer in Peterborough, while Rob and Gerald force a curry restaurant in Manchester to close early. Andy plans to remove two boats from Falmouth Harbour to cover an unpaid debt, and the sheriffs visit a pub on the Isle of Wight to retrieve money on behalf of a former employee. Narrated by David Reed

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: A team of three players must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Fine art dealer Gavin Claxton, trader Melissa Downhill and upcycler Zoe Pocock head to Chipping Campden, Gloucestershire, to search for bargains, competing to make the most money

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George and Fiona go away for a romantic weekend, only to learn things are not going according to plan back home as the fire brigade pays Arlo a visit. Meanwhile, Jan has some surprising news of her own

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the nine remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges for sportswear week. To test the sewers' ability to create practical and stylish sportswear, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young kick off the pattern challenge with a rugby shirt. In the transformation challenge, the contestants turn cagoules into miniature waterproof onesies for toddlers, before taking on the difficult task of making tennis outfits in the made-to-measure challenge

programme.title: Italy's Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott explore the Italian city of Venice, using 3D technology to discover how a city built in a swamp came to be one of the most powerful locations in Medieval Europe. The pair dive deep into the many canals in this unique place to experience how its buildings still remain standing to this day, and highlight how Venice's beauty once masked a ruthless secret state, and a world of excess and vice

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Michael Baggott assess objects brought into the University of Nottingham, while presenter Paul Martin learns about a Nottingham-born Victorian prize-fighter called Bendigo and meets locals who are keeping his memory alive

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jayde Adams, Josie d'Arby, Rufus Hound and Scott Mills test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal dishes more backstage gossip, talks to more couples and has a sneak peek at a few of Saturday's costumes with designer Vicky Gill

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Powering Britain
programme.desc: Keeley Donovan meets the army of people working to meet the country's energy demands. She begins 75 miles off the Yorkshire coast at the world's biggest offshore windfarm. Hornsea One has broken every record for its size and scale - with 10,000 people involved in its construction in the North Sea. Keeley meets some of the staff who made it happen - but can she get to the top of one of the 174 turbines in the field?

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan and Gillian Burke return with more live wildlife reports

programme.title: The Trump Show
programme.desc: The story of Donald Trump's fourth year in office, when the president, emboldened by his exoneration in the Russia investigation, invited the Taliban to Camp David and suggested buying Greenland from Denmark. It was also the year that Covid-19 spread around the world. In America, the virus killed more than 150,000 people by the middle of the year - yet Trump pushed back against government scientists. And when police officers killed George Floyd, the violent protests that followed forced Trump to an underground bunker. Now, as the election nears, the programme explores whether Trump has lost control of the narrative or whether his political-savvy fighting instincts will propel him to another victory

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Maisie Adam, Eshaan Akbar, Ed Byrne, Sophie Duker and Ed Gamble. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Highlights from the satirical news show, presented by Nish Kumar, as a cast of comedy correspondents give their take on the events from May 2019

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A 75-year-old patient has an enlarged aorta, the main vessel for carrying oxygenated blood to the body. Her condition could cause her to die at any time - but treating it also poses a risk to her life. At Addenbrooke's hospital, a patient with a genetic condition that makes her susceptible to cancer undergoes treatment to remove a polyp that has the potential to turn into cancer

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael Palin looks back on the making of Pole to Pole, in which he travelled along a single line of longitude across the USSR, Africa and finally into Antarctica. Along the way, he paid a visit to Chernobyl and gained an insight into the last days of the Soviet Union. His travels across Africa led to an encounter with some fellow globetrotters from his home town of Sheffield - but he was forced to make a detour when his planned route passed through a war zone

programme.title: In the Face of Terror
programme.desc: Nearly five years on from Jim Foley's execution at the hands of Jihadi John, Diane Foley is fighting for justice, while the Muellers continue their search for answers about their missing daughter Kayla. In London a court case in the UK Supreme Court threatens Diane's campaign to see the suspects face a trial in the US, while the Muellers are conducting their own investigation, supported by contacts working secretly in Syria

programme.title: War on Plastic with Hugh and Anita
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Anita Rani present an update on the campaign against single use plastic, challenging a British family from Berwick to go plastic free on a budget. Hugh discovers what becomes of the packaging for pre-packed sandwiches, and Anita conducts experiments on teabags to discover the secrets of their construction. The programme also examines how the Coronavirus pandemic has led to a rise in single use plastic for safety reasons

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: A tenant making more than £100,000 unlawfully sub-letting his council flat, and a criminal gang running a multimillion-pound diesel fraud

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours the skips at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey for things to restore and sell for a profit. Items include a pair of leather wallets and a rocking chair

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four family heirlooms back to life. Fine art conservator Lucia Scalisi restores a treasured painting by celebrated artist CJ Frost, while furniture restorer Will Kirk sets to work on an antique sewing table. Elsewhere, Steve Fletcher and David Burville repair a 130-year-old automaton tiger, and fountain pen restorer Laurence Oldfield tackles the restoration of a vintage Wyvern fountain pen

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: Three people who have never met before must work as a team and answer questions correctly in a bid to win a share of a £20,000 cash prize. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Antiques expert Caroline Hawley, trader Gary Barton and furniture designer Nessa Doran O'Reilly head to Whitchurch to find bargains they can turn into profitfor Children in Need

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Jan's pregnancy threatens to drive a wedge between George and Fiona, while demand for paintings by Dennis is outstripping supply, so Shay comes up with an ingenious solution

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the eight remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges as they return to the sewing room for lingerie and sleepwear week. To test the sewers' handling skills, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young kick off the pattern challenge with a boned basque. The task requires sewing together small pattern pieces for a precise fit and creating a tricky hook and eye fastening. Next, in the transformation challenge, the sewers transform pyjamas and nightwear into a summer dress, before finally being tasked to create a two-piece female sleep set which has to incorporates lace somewhere in their design

programme.title: Italy's Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Using new 3D technology, Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott explore the Italian city of Florence, and with the help of historian Ross King and the team, they discover the hidden secrets of Florence's great cathedral and its architectural genius. The pair also learn more about the Medici's fraught relationship with Michelangelo, as he fell from favour when he supported a rebellion against his own masters, and visit the world-famous Uffizi - including the Botticelli room

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Culzean Castle on the west coast of Scotland in Ayrshire, experts Anita Manning and Natasha Raskin Sharp are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. A watercolour with a local connection catches Anita's eye, while Natasha is drawn to a gorgeous handwoven blanket. Paul Martin explores a hidden part of Culzean's history, uncovering the tales of smugglers working the coastline in the caves beneath the castle walls

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jayde Adams, Josie d'Arby, Rufus Hound and Scott Mills test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games. This edition sees the week's overall winner crowned

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal presents analysis of the couples' progress as they prepare for tomorrow night's show. Including reports from the studio rehearsals and interviews with celebrity fans

programme.title: Simply Nigella
programme.desc: Food writer Nigella Lawson examines how different days require certain types of food, sharing her favourite menu to help get that balance right in the kitchen. She prepares a light salad of salmon, avocado, watercress and pumpkin seeds, and a chicken shawarma with an array of spices, sauces and flatbreads - a dish inspired by a spit-roasted Lebanese kebab that is perfect for feeding a crowd. Nigella also creates two sweet treats, a glossy liquorice and blackcurrant chocolate cake, and chocolate chip cookie dough pots served with ice-cream or creme fraiche

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox is joined by comedians Jo Brand and Andi Osho, actor and comedian Adrian Edmondson and singer Will Young, who share their favourite books as well as revealing the books that remind them of a particular person and their recommended non-fiction reads. They all discuss this week's book group picks - This Lovely City by Louise Hare and Will's own book, To Be a Gay Man

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: In a Halloween-themed programme, Gillian Burke shows off the amazing hydraulic power of spiders' legs. Iolo Williams has an exclusive update on the remarkable story of a giant vulture that set up home in Derbyshire, Megan McCubbin and Chris Packham look back at the mouse experiment and their friendly foxes, and Michaela Strachan catches up on the highs and lows of the Scottish seal colony

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don harvests his pumpkins at Longmeadow, plants bulbs in a pot and has plenty of jobs for everyone to be getting on with over the coming weeks. Frances Tophill travels to Winkworth Arboretum in Surrey to celebrate the season and to explain the science behind the golden and red tones of autumn leaves. The team take inspiration from RHS Harlow Carr and take a look at their winter borders and the plants which will add interest and drama to gardens over the coming months

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools chats to Pulp frontman Jarvis Cocker about his musical journey, illustrated by a selection of clips from the Later archive. No stranger to Later and the Hootenanny, Cocker has performed on the show five times, the last in 2006 with music from his debut solo release. There are also performances by debutants Greentea Peng and Working Men's Club

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: The ship manages to rejoin the fleet, but at a heavy price - a Cylon computer virus has found its way into the Galactica's system. Colonel Tigh fears the Centurions are planning to murder the crew and seize control of the vessel. Meanwhile, Number Six gives Baltar some unnerving news

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: While Adama recovers from surgery after being shot, the crash survivors on Kobol learn of an anti-aircraft weapon deployed by the Cylons to take out the rescue ship. Tigh realises the magnitude of his role as stand-in Commander when the Quorum of Twelve challenges his authority

programme.title: The Awakening (2011)
programme.desc: In 1920s England, paranormal investigator Florence Cathcart travels around the country exposing supposed supernatural occurrences as hoaxes. She is summoned to a remote boarding school to examine reports of a haunting, but finds her rational disbelief in the occult challenged by the disturbing events that unfold there. Horror, starring Rebecca Hall, Dominic West and Imelda Staunton

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Coronavirus has hit the whole nation hard, but for the under-25s the impact has been particularly difficult. Reporter Kash Jones investigates the long-term consequences of the pandemic on young people, revealing exclusive research about the impact of Covid-19 on them and the scale of the challenges ahead. He meets young adults trying to deal with the impact on their education, job prospects and mental health

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: Surgeons at Royal Papworth work to completely rebuild the chest wall of a patient who had to have a cancerous lung removed, and has lived with a hole in his back ever since. Consultant thoracic surgeon Aman Coonar and plastic surgeon Charles Malata must take a flap of muscle and skin from Kevin's back and painstakingly attach the blood vessels to fill the cavity and plug the hole. At Addenbrooke, a leading neurosurgeon must remove a potentially fatal tumour from the back of a patient's brain

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: The former steeplejack examines the development of castles from early Iron Age forts to secret tunnels used during the Second World War. He visits Hadrian's Wall, where he takes a look at the remains of Roman toilets, while in Warwick a band of medieval knights tests the castle's defences

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as demonstrates his painting technique, beginning with a picture of mountains, evergreens, paths and bushes

programme.title: The Fairytale Castles of King Ludwig II with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the aesthetic styles associated with Ludwig II of Bavaria, the king who was considered to be handsome and loved by his people. Dan Cruickshank investigates the mock-medievalism of Neuschwanstein castle and the complex design of Herrenchiemsee, to assess how they are key to learning more about the enigmatic ruler

programme.title: The Fall of the Berlin Wall with John Simpson
programme.desc: To mark the 30th anniversary of the collapse of the Berlin Wall on November 9, 1989, John Simpson, the BBC's longest-serving correspondent, goes back to his reports on what he believes to be the most important story he has ever covered. Back then, John thought the even would change the world for the better, for ever. Three decades on, he looks at how history has actually turned out and whether he was wrong to have been so optimistic

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Berlin Wall
programme.desc: Originally shown to mark 20 years since the structure that divided the city was brought down, this documentary hears from the people most closely involved with the horrors it created. Spies, informers, double-agents and interrogators explain their roles in the corruption and brutalisation of the new socialist world the Wall's designers had thought they were forging

programme.title: Blood of the Clans
programme.desc: Neil Oliver uncovers the story of Rob Roy MacGregor, who has for centuries been celebrated as a Highland maverick, as a well-intentioned rogue - Scotland's answer to Robin Hood

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as demonstrates his painting technique, beginning with a picture of mountains, evergreens, paths and bushes

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: The former steeplejack examines the development of castles from early Iron Age forts to secret tunnels used during the Second World War. He visits Hadrian's Wall, where he takes a look at the remains of Roman toilets, while in Warwick a band of medieval knights tests the castle's defences

programme.title: The Fairytale Castles of King Ludwig II with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the aesthetic styles associated with Ludwig II of Bavaria, the king who was considered to be handsome and loved by his people. Dan Cruickshank investigates the mock-medievalism of Neuschwanstein castle and the complex design of Herrenchiemsee, to assess how they are key to learning more about the enigmatic ruler

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Berlin Wall
programme.desc: Originally shown to mark 20 years since the structure that divided the city was brought down, this documentary hears from the people most closely involved with the horrors it created. Spies, informers, double-agents and interrogators explain their roles in the corruption and brutalisation of the new socialist world the Wall's designers had thought they were forging

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: The former steeplejack celebrates Britain's houses and palaces, travelling from the grandeur of Hampton Court in west London to a homely terrace in Beamish in Co Durham. He also visits Cragside, Northumberland, one of the first homes to have electric lighting, and helps restore a 15th-century manor in Kent by demonstrating how plaster used to be made

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an American south western mountain scene, with red peaks looming over a lazy, tree-lined stream

programme.title: A Star Is Born (1954)
programme.desc: A Hollywood movie star with a drinking problem is captivated by an aspiring singer's performance. He falls in love with her and helps her become a star, but as her career blossoms, his falls into ruin. Musical drama, starring Judy Garland, James Mason, Jack Carson and Charles Bickford. Directed by George Cukor, with songs by Harold Arlen and Ira Gershwin

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic dissects his favourite genre, horror, and reveals how film-makers exploit people's darkest fears. He features examples from Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, Jordan Peele's surprise blockbuster Get Out and the movie that has become Mark's lifetime's obsession, William Friedkin's The Exorcist

programme.title: Big Sky, Big Dreams, Big Art: Made in the USA
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak focuses on the America of small towns and trailer parks, discovering how these much-maligned territories had an immensely beneficial impact on American art. From the small-town brilliance of Grant Wood, to the small-town alienation of Edward Hopper, to the spooky Dust Bowl symbolism of Alexandre Hogue, interstitial America inspired much that was great. The film culminates in the assemblages of David Smith, the leading sculptor of Abstract Expressionism

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an American south western mountain scene, with red peaks looming over a lazy, tree-lined stream

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: The former steeplejack celebrates Britain's houses and palaces, travelling from the grandeur of Hampton Court in west London to a homely terrace in Beamish in Co Durham. He also visits Cragside, Northumberland, one of the first homes to have electric lighting, and helps restore a 15th-century manor in Kent by demonstrating how plaster used to be made

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic dissects his favourite genre, horror, and reveals how film-makers exploit people's darkest fears. He features examples from Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, Jordan Peele's surprise blockbuster Get Out and the movie that has become Mark's lifetime's obsession, William Friedkin's The Exorcist

programme.title: Frankenstein: How to Make a Monster
programme.desc: In BAC Beatbox Academy's hit show, six performers interpret Mary Shelley's novel Frankenstein from their own perspective as young people growing up in 21st-century Britain. This musical film is part performance, part documentary, with the cast's voices as the only instruments

programme.title: The Secret Life of Books
programme.desc: Anatomist Alice Roberts explores Mary Shelley's Frankenstein, questioning the main themes of the novel and asking who could have influenced the author's vision of the story. Plus, the presenter travels to the Villa Diodati in Geneva, where Shelley first conceived the idea of Victor Frankenstein's creature, before examining the original manuscript at the Bodleian Library in Oxford

programme.title: The Sound of Movie Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: Once the Second World War ended, the American musical began to thrive with a colour and exuberance even more pronounced than before, entering a second Golden Age. Central to its success, the composer and musician argues, was the emergence of Gene Kelly. Talking with Kelly's widow, Patricia Ward Kelly, Brand looks at how this star brought the musical film into the streets of America, most famously with Singin' in the Rain, believed to be one of the greatest films ever made of any genre. Brand also takes a look at significant Indian movies of this period, as well as some made in China

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Anthea Turner hosts the edition first aired on February 15, with performances by artists including Sinead O'Connor, The Wedding Present and Black Box

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the edition first aired on February 22, with performances by artists including Tina Turner, Beats International and Adam Ant

programme.title: Pop Charts Britannia: 60 Years of the Top 10
programme.desc: The evolution of feelings and attitudes toward the British singles chart. Documenting changes from the 1952 NME countdown via Pick and Top of the Pops through to the Radio 1 chart show of 2012. With contributions from chart show presenters David Jacobs and Reggie Yates, music fans Grace Dent and Pete Paphides and veteran musicians Jon Webster and Rob Dickins

programme.title: A Musical History
programme.desc: A multi-faceted genius whose music has permeated popular culture, Stevie Wonder is not short of celebrity fans. Here, some well-known fans celebrate the man and his music by selecting some of his best-loved songs. Contributors include actor Martin Freeman, singers Alexander O'Neal, James Morrison, Beverley Knight and Corinne Bailey Rae, New Order's Gillian Gilbert and Stephen Morris, DJs Ana Matronic, Trevor Nelson and Norman Jay, Heaven's 17's Glenn Gregory and Martyn Ware, journalist Sian Pattenden and presenter Emma Dabiri

programme.title: Sings Stevie Wonder
programme.desc: A trawl through the BBC archives, unearthing 50 years of covers of Wonder's classic songbook, featuring artists including Cilla Black, Jimmy Helms, Dionne Warwick, the Osmonds, India Arie and James Morrison. Among the clips is a storming performance of Ed Sheeran with Jools Holland and his Rhythm & Blues Orchestra taking on Master Blaster (Jammin')

programme.title: Radio 2 in Concert
programme.desc: The singer-songwriter performs an intimate concert at the Maida Vale Studios in London. Featuring new songs from Still on My Mind, along with hits from No Angel and Life for Rent

programme.desc: Find out why living in slow motion would let you see things no-one else can!

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Zionne, Isaiah and Mandi are young black teenagers who have all experienced racism growing up in the UK. Mandi has been called racist names, Isaiah is often followed round shops by security guards, and Zionne feels she will always have to work harder than everyone else to succeed. In the first of a two-part special, they speak out about what life is really like growing up black in the UK and why things urgently need to change. After the death of George Floyd and the nationwide protests against racism, the teenagers know that this is their time to be heard. As well as opening up about their own experiences, they speak to their friends and their parents. They decide to create a manifesto to list the big things they feel need to change in society to make life better for black kids like them.

programme.desc: Formula One superstar Lewis Hamilton joins three black teenagers on a mission to make life better for black kids growing up in the UK. Zionne, Isaiah and Mandi have all experienced racism and have come together to create a manifesto to list the big changes they want to see in society. They enlist their celebrity heroes Lewis Hamilton, Nissy Tee and DJ Sideman to help get their message out there and discover that even the most successful black people have had to overcome racism along the way. Inspired by their role models, they are determined to make a change

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: Mr Malone holds a mock trial to resolve a dispute Martha and Ollie are having over the ownership of Edison, a cuddly lightbulb they won together at a fairground hoopla. Rufus is appointed Ollie’s ineffective lawyer. (His amenability is something his girlfriend Kat finds very frustrating). Lily is Martha’s disappointing lawyer - whilst promising an epic opening statement, Lily makes it all about Rob being in America. Ollie asks Martha to prove she can throw a hoop on target. Martha objects, but Judge Kat insists that it will be amusing to witness Martha throw hoops. Martha applies the scientific calculations she gave to Ollie to help him win Edison, but she still fails to throw a hoop successfully. Ollie nears victory when the court adjourns because Mr Malone is hungry. Later, whilst Rufus admits to Kat that he doesn’t feel he is cut out to be a lawyer, he offers Kat the largest slice of cake even though he wants it. This infuriates Kat. She tells Rufus that he needs to be less easy-goi

programme.desc: It’s Halloween and Mimi is on a mission to prank her best friend, Ashleigh, who claims she is not afraid of anything. She has found a spooky cave rumoured to be haunted by a ghost searching for his own heart, which was stolen several hundred years ago. Mimi also introduces sisters Angel, Melody and Nevaeh, who are super-proud of their traveller heritage and go trick or treating on their plot of land. Another Halloween fan is TikTok phenomenon Fats Timbo, who is Mimi’s Q&A guest and tells us how she has managed to use her platform to empower herself and others. Ashleigh is unimpressed when she finally arrives at the cave - until a surprise visitor gives her the fright of her life.

programme.title: Bitz & Bob

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Things turn messy when Robert invents a cake-making machine. Guest starring Katy Ashworth, with Justin Fletcher

programme.title: CBeebies' The Snow Queen
programme.desc: Brave village girl Gerda sets out to rescue her best friend Kai before the Snow Queen freezes him for ever. Cat Sandion, William Vanderpuye, Ranj Singh, Rebecca Keatley, Steven Kynman, Andy Day, Jennie Dale, Richard David-Caine, Joseph Elliott, Gemma Hunt, Justin Fletcher and Maddie Moate star in this stage musical adaptation of the tale by Hans Christian Andersen, containing five new songs

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Fun and games for youngsters with the playful quartet

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels want to play outside on a very windy day, and Duggee suggests they all go fly his kite

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: It's the day of Mrs Twirlyhorn's nature walk, but Wufflebump has forgotten all his nature-finding equipment. So, after a trip to the lost property cupboard, he comes up with some unusual and inventive ways of studying the wonders of nature

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: Sid becomes a ballroom dancer, and pairs up with the talented Roxy Trots to enter a competition

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk through the docks to get on board an old sailing ship.

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: The youngster splashes in a puddle, making little spots of light dance about on the surface, and everyone tries to catch the Sparkles

programme.title: Tik Tak
programme.desc: Explore a mosaic of a crab and a dissection puzzle of a lamp, and watch as Book Boy makes a ball.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Brothers Bobby and Lenny are busy making robot costumes out of cardboard boxes

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: A photographer comes to Tig's nursery, but she refuses to smile for any of the photos because she is grumpy

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: A talented young cook from the Midlands prepares a very special Bakewell tart for his friends

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Doug's mum, Zoe, brings surprises for the Brooklyn-Bells, but Waffle is sad because Doug is going to stay with her for a few days. Mrs Hobbs watches Waffle with new spy equipment

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Timmy gets dirty by jumping into puddles, kicking muddy footballs and getting splattered by jam sandwiches just before the class photograph

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Katie worries about Grannie Island, who having hurt her back and unable to get out of bed, does not want to trouble anyone. The youngster decides to ask Granma Mainland for help

programme.title: Melody
programme.desc: Mum and Melody find a pile of building blocks in the park and decide to have a competition to see who can build the taller tower

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee insists on wearing his favourite Action Ape outfit all afternoon, no matter what games he is playing. However, when the outfit gets dirty, Tee is heartbroken

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: O meets a wig and thinks it is the cutest thing he has ever seen, and he is overjoyed when he discovers that it goes on his head

programme.title: Yakka Dee!

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny makes his own magic stone when Sula won't involve him in her game - but disaster strikes

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: It's a grey and rainy day in the real world and Messy can see something amazing outside his window. A beautiful arch of colours magically appears across the sky

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dispy, Laa-Laa and Po have their favourite things. The bag, the hat, the scooter and the ball all magically swap places and become mixed up

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Rory visits Porter and his pet bearded dragon, Beardy, who has a poorly tummy and needs a trip to the vet. Ferne joins a mobile vet on the Isle of Wight, where they visit lots of pets who need care

programme.title: Our Family

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day travels back in time after promising to get some more salt for Hatty so she can finish her display of a fossilised footprint of an allosaurus, a massive meat-eating dinosaur that roamed the salt flats of North America 145 million years ago. Along the way, he befriends one of the creatures and gets caught up in chase along the salt flats as they dodge the giant legs and long necks of a herd of diplodocus

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Bob, or Big Bad Robo Wolf, showing just how bad he is, locks Ra-Purl-Zel in the castle tower. While Purl is busily knitting, Bitz and Sir Bevel try to engineer a way to rescue her

programme.title: Go Jetters

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran are getting ready for a Halloween party, but when JoJo accidentally squashes their spooky pumpkin lantern, they need a replacement! Luckily Gran Gran has a Gran Gran Plan to save the day.

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: The spider shows Tig how sharing can be fun when her friend comes round to play

programme.title: Clangers

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Pontipines eat their dinner in the Ninky Nonk, but bounce all around the garden

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Tonight's Bedtime Story, told by Shauna Shim, is all about a friendly witch called Witchety Sticks. Witchety Sticks keeps magic buttons in her pockets. Sometimes her magic works and sometimes it doesn't. When a lost Littlepotamus can't find his way home, Witchety Sticks's magic buttons come to the rescue, but can her magic get Littlepotamus home?

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Heavy snow disrupts plans for the wedding as travel to Button House becomes near impossible, so Alison and Mike have to rally round to make sure Sam and Clare's big day goes ahead. The Captain is in his element with the emergency preparations to the house, but Pat is distracted by an unexpected reminder of his past, while Lady Button is aghast at what Alison is allowing to take place at her former home

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Ceramics conservator Kirsten Ramsay is charged with the task of repairing a little cat teapot with an extraordinary wartime story behind it, while Dominic Chinea takes on the restoration of a once brightly coloured wooden carousel horse. The team also brings a wooden boat puzzle and a Victorian magic lantern back to life

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Crewmates Adam and Dan treat a patient who has fallen down a flight of stairs in the casino, while Rachel and Stuart respond to a call when a child falls from a bunk bed. programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Honey's date takes a terrifying turn when unbeknown to her, Paul spikes her drink. Ruby tells Kush to return the money he stole and she will forget it ever happened - but he denies it, leaving her furious and determined to get revenge on the Slaters. Shirley tries to talk to Mick, but he pushes her too far by saying she was never there for him

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg and Chris are in Bromley with the Winbourne family, who desperately want to change their ways. Busy mum Clare and daughters Rachel and Sadie all rely on dad James to cook the family meals, but not everyone is satisfied with what's being rustled up. With the same meals on rotation and a lack of fruit and veg being consumed, the whole family are bored when it comes to mealtimes, and often resort to eating out

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at the interview stage and a helpful guide on how to survive the process, which sees the last few candidates go toe-to-toe with Alan Sugar's trusted business associates - Claude Littner, Linda Plant and Mike Soutar - as their CVs and business plans are heavily scrutinised

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the audience. Presented by Fiona Bruce

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Another chance to this intimate portrait of an exceptionally gifted musical family in lockdown, the Kanneh-Masons. Ever since lockdown began, the seven young prodigies aged 10 to 24 have been isolated in the family home in Nottingham, along with their parents Stuart and Kadiatu. Unable to perform publicly, the family decided to stage a vibrant and eclectic concert in the only place they can - their own home - and granted the BBC exclusive access using fixed rig cameras installed in the house that were remotely operated
