programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: A family consider moving to Australia to help their son, who has a rare genetic disorder. Though mum Jayne believes sunshine will help young Albie, dad Eugene needs convincing

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: In Birmingham, the investigators crack down on the callous travel agent ripping off British holidaymakers, taking their money but never booking their flights. In light of the story of one limo driving a school party of 11-year-olds bursting into flames, there's also some spot-checking on luxury limos being driven in London

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In a rock and pop special, Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to the iconic BBC Maida Vale music studios, and gets the lowdown on an incredible collection of music memorabilia

programme.desc: Customers unbox, test and score a lint roller that specialises in pet hair, a storage solution for tin cans, and a plastic stick that can be shaped to help with various DIY tasks

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Sonali Shah makes a beeline for the desirable town of Sevenoaks in Kent, to help buyers who are selling up their London flat and sinking their £900,000 budget into a new life

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore searches for tired and unwanted items at Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey she can transform from skip-bound to stylish. With three items saved, Sarah turns to upholsterer Sharon O'Connor to overhaul an old chair frame, while upcycler Jacqui Joseph attempts to transform a dated desk. Sarah's challenge is to update a set of four chairs. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers and valuation expert Simon Bower welcome four more people with interesting items they'd like to sell. Nigel then invites the dealers to bid against one another to secure a vintage fairground game, a 19th-century Chinese inkwell, a 1950s toy pedal car and a Victorian watchman's clock

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances. Presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker hosts the light-hearted quiz, with Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell heading teams of celebrities in a test of their sporting knowledge. Taking part this time around are horse racing legend AP McCoy, World Cup-winning cricketer Charlotte Edwards, former England goalkeeper David James and Olympic cycling champion Joanna Rowsell Shand

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedian and writer David Baddiel, former MP and Strictly Come Dancing 2016 star Ed Balls, stand-up and actress Jo Brand and ex-Pussycat Doll and Celebrity MasterChef 2015 winner Kimberly Wyatt join host Rob Brydon and captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Suki proposes a poker game with Vinny and Kheerat at Ruby's club and suggests they invite Kush, but he is suspicious of her motive and declines. However, when he later turns up he finds them ready to play - and cannot resist joining in. Meanwhile, Shirley offers Gray a job in the Mitchell family business

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Channel 4 presenter Steph McGovern hosts the satirical quiz, inviting comedian Miles Jupp and Baroness Sayeeda Warsi to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: Rhod trains to become a fighter pilot with the RAF, which is quite possibly his toughest and most frightening work experience yet, as the closest he has got to a jet up until now is watching Top Gun. His fears are realised when it comes to his final challenge - co-piloting on an exercise

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham is joined by Irish actor and singer Jessie Buckley, starring in Charlie Kaufman's Netflix drama I'm Thinking of Ending Things. Also appearing are comedian Bill Bailey - one of this year's Strictly contestants - plus fellow comic and children's author David Walliams. Singer Dermot Kennedy performs his single Giants, and the host offers a wry look at some trending news stories

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce is challenged to live with a capsule wardrobe - selecting just 14 items from her vast collection of clothes and wearing nothing else for two weeks. Busy mum Clare and daughters Rachel and Sadie all rely on dad James to cook the family meals, but not everyone is satisfied with what's being rustled up. With the same meals on rotation and a lack of fruit and veg being consumed, the whole family are bored when it comes to mealtimes, and often resort to eating out

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at the interview stage and a helpful guide on how to survive the process, which sees the last few candidates go toe-to-toe with Alan Sugar's trusted business associates - Claude Littner, Linda Plant and Mike Soutar - as their CVs and business plans are heavily scrutinised

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the audience. Presented by Fiona Bruce

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Another chance to this intimate portrait of an exceptionally gifted musical family in lockdown, the Kanneh-Masons. Ever since lockdown began, the seven young prodigies aged 10 to 24 have been isolated in the family home in Nottingham, along with their parents Stuart and Kadiatu. Unable to perform publicly, the family decided to stage a vibrant and eclectic concert in the only place they can - their own home - and granted the BBC exclusive access using fixed rig cameras installed in the house that were remotely operated

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the development progress of properties in Pimlico, London, Ilkeston, Derbyshire and Chesterton, near Newcastle-under-Lyme

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: In Birmingham, the investigators crack down on the callous travel agent ripping off British holidaymakers, taking their money but never booking their flights. In light of the story of one limo driving a school party of 11-year-olds bursting into flames, there's also some spot-checking on luxury limos being driven in London

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A couple whose car was hit by a truck on the M1 while on a journey to celebrate their wedding anniversary. Plus, a wing suit free-fall enthusiast who hit trouble when his main parachute became tangled up, 30 seconds from the ground. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Coronavirus: Scotland's Response
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Scottish Government's Briefing with reaction, expert analysis and the very latest updates from Scotland and beyond on the pandemic

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers unbox, test and score a lint roller that specialises in pet hair, a storage solution for tin cans, and a plastic stick that can be shaped to help with various DIY tasks

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Sonali Shah makes a beeline for the desirable town of Sevenoaks in Kent, to help buyers who are selling up their London flat and sinking their £900,000 budget into a new life

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore searches for tired and unwanted items at Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey she can transform from skip-bound to stylish. With three items saved, Sarah turns to upholsterer Sharon O'Connor to overhaul an old chair frame, while upcycler Jacqui Joseph attempts to transform a dated desk. Sarah's challenge is to update a set of four chairs. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers and valuation expert Simon Bower welcome four more people with interesting items they'd like to sell. Nigel then invites the dealers to bid against one another to secure a vintage fairground game, a 19th-century Chinese inkwell, a 1950s toy pedal car and a Victorian watchman's clock

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances. Presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond is in the Cairngorms hoping to see the annual red deer rut. Euan McIlwraith celebrates the colour red in the landscape and Arlene Stuart is picking acorns

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker hosts the light-hearted quiz, with Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell heading teams of celebrities in a test of their sporting knowledge. Taking part this time around are horse racing legend AP McCoy, World Cup-winning cricketer Charlotte Edwards, former England goalkeeper David James and Olympic cycling champion Joanna Rowsell Shand

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Suki proposes a poker game with Vinny and Kheerat at Ruby's club and suggests they invite Kush, but he is suspicious of her motive and declines. However, when he later turns up he finds them ready to play - and cannot resist joining in. Meanwhile, Shirley offers Gray a job in the Mitchell family business

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Channel 4 presenter Steph McGovern hosts the satirical quiz, inviting comedian Miles Jupp and Baroness Sayeeda Warsi to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: With Dave and Denise's wedding fast approaching, the arrival of a huge phone bill is the last thing Jim needs. Another chance to see the first-ever episode of the award-winning comedy, starring Caroline Aherne, Craig Cash, Ricky Tomlinson and Sue Johnston

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham is joined by Irish actor and singer Jessie Buckley, starring in Charlie Kaufman's Netflix drama I'm Thinking of Ending Things. Also appearing are comedian Bill Bailey - one of this year's Strictly contestants - plus fellow comic and children's author David Walliams. Singer Dermot Kennedy performs his single Giants, and the host offers a wry look at some trending news stories

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Edmund and Nora prepare to launch their app, and Wally's dinner date goes terribly awry

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: After Scrubr gets acquired by a company overseas, Nora moves to China. Back in Queens, Grandma befriends an injured pigeon

programme.title: A View from the Terrace
programme.desc: Craig G Telfer, Craig Fowler, Shaughan McGuigan and Joel Sked present a sideways look at Scottish football, dissecting of ins and outs of the week's action, with a variety of features covering the full spectrum from the giants to the minnows

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host this weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore a large ground-floor flat in Thornton Heath, Croydon, a former restaurant in Maidstone, Kent, and a terraced house in Liverpool

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers hunt for counterfeit and dangerous goods at Suffolk's Felixstowe Port. Elsewhere, a gang who callously dumped tons of illegal waste are tracked down

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A father of five is left fighting for his life after collapsing at a trampoline park, and a paraglider gets into trouble when he loses height at an alarming rate

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams test their antiques knowledge in Brighton with the help of experts Danny Sebastian and John Cameron. Presented by Charlie Ross, who also commemorates the 75th anniversary of D-Day with a visit to the D-Day Story in Portsmouth

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers unbox, test and score a vegan skincare range, a coat for drying dogs, and a device designed to attach items of hand luggage together

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple who want to swap life in a hamlet outside Chester for an attractive Suffolk town

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades and a team of experts transform a pair of chairs, a collection of metal racks and drawers, and a school desk at Walsall Recycling Centre

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are offered to chance to bid on a vintage table hockey game, an early-20th-century glass chandelier and antique sketch books that belonged to the artist Henry Thomson

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio, presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: David Jason returns as Granville in this follow-up to Roy Clarke's popular comedy Open All Hours, as he re-opens the doors of the nation's favourite corner shop. In the opening episode, Arkwright's protege finds an old mangle in the yard - and wonders how he can best put it to use. Meanwhile, Beth continues to pressure Leroy to find a date for her best friend, Ruby, who has been suspended at work. Elsewhere, Eric and Cyril tutor Mr Newbold in the art of romance - and how to get closer to Mrs Rossi. Co-starring James Baxter, Johnny Vegas and Katie Redford

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Ceramics conservator Kirsten Ramsay is charged with the task of repairing a little cat teapot with an extraordinary wartime story behind it, while Dominic Chinea takes on the restoration of a once brightly coloured wooden carousel horse. The team also brings a wooden boat puzzle and a Victorian magic lantern back to life

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Crewmates Adam and Dan treat a patient who has fallen down a flight of stairs in the casino, while Rachel and Stuart respond to a call when a child falls from a bunk bed. As the shift continues, a distressing call from a patient threatening to jump into the river takes priority, and the call handler must try to keep the patient talking until help can arrive

programme.title: Wee Chef!
programme.desc: Part one of two. Following young apprentices from Belfast's Metropolitan College professional cookery course, as they learn basic skills and experience the heat of a real kitchen

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: Six unsigned MCs compete to become the next rap superstar, as they go head-to-head in a battle to sign for rap legends Krept and Konan's record label. In the first episode of the new series, the artists perform for the first time in front of rap legends Krept, Konan, and DJ Target, demonstrating their A Capella skills, before rapping about a key moment in their life to guest mentor Rapman

programme.title: Rap Trip: Underground Scenes Uncovered
programme.desc: Rappers FOS and Ransom travel to Liverpool to learn about Scouse Rap. They meet a 16-year-old female drill artist and a rapper from the Wirral who raps about his autism

programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: A tenant making more than £100,000 unlawfully subletting his council flat, and a criminal gang running a multimillion-pound diesel fraud

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A truck driver is diverted along a narrow road that cannot cope with the weight of his vehicle, with disastrous consequences. Paramedics treat an unresponsive baby

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Presenter Anita Manning leads proceedings at the Yorkshire Events Centre in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, where Kate Bliss and Mark Stacey help two teams to find bargains to take to auction for a profit. programme.title: The Debate Continues
programme.desc: Shown exactly 70 years after it was first transmitted in 1950, this historic film documents the restoration of the House of Commons which was severely damaged by a bombing raid in May 1941. There is rare footage of King George VI delivering an address to both Houses of Parliament in Westminster Hall to mark the reopening of the restored House of Commons. First shown on the BBC Television Service on 28 October 1950.

programme.title: Inspector Morse

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The last run of the detective drama ended with Lewis (Kevin Whately) starting a new life away from the force, but it seems that peace and quiet doesn't suit him - which is just as well, as newly promoted DI Hathaway (Laurence Fox) is struggling to find a sidekick, and within just four weeks is already on to his second sergeant, DS Lizzie Maddox (Angela Griffin). So, Chief Superintendent Innocent (Rebecca Front) decides to reunite the former partners to look into the murder of a neurosurgeon, a case with potential links to the worlds of animal rights and blood sports. In the first of this two-part story, suspicion falls on glamorous widow Erica (Kara Tointon), but it turns out there are plenty of other people in the victim's inner circle who were driven by fear and loathing

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: With the surveillance teams hot on their tails, the gang members squabble among themselves over who should receive a cut of the takings. Will he cut Reader in on the deal? Timothy Spall, Kenneth Cranham and David Hayman star in the fact-based drama

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police are called in to investigate when a local judge is the target of a series of mysterious attacks, but the case takes a sinister turn when the prime suspect is found dead on his own doorstep. Peggy is delighted when David's new radio starts picking up police broadcasts, but a news reporter doesn't share her enthusiasm. Rural drama, with Gwen Taylor and David Lonsdale

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned is angry with Jan for forcing Jack to leave. Kim invites Sophie to come with her to New Zealand. Zoe tells Sophie that if she leaves she need not come back

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sophie tells Zoe she has changed her mind about not wanting a baby with her. Sam decides to give himself up to the police. Viv flirts with Paddy when they get drunk together

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen knows Bill is to be evicted and wonders why he has not mentioned it. Roy surprises Gail and Martin by agreeing to buy the cafe. Judy discovers she is not pregnant

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Judy tells Gary she is not pregnant and he wonders if he is sterile. Deirdre feels Ken is taking it for granted that she will move in with him

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the apparent suicide of a solicitor rumoured to have been stealing money from clients. When rival lawyer Otto Benham is later found murdered in a bizarre manner, the detectives begin to suspect the two deaths may be related. Mystery, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Barry Jackson, James Weber Brown and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective faces a tough time while looking into a murder at a peaceful college. After suffering the familiar pangs of unrequited love, he clashes with the unpleasant college master and then unwittingly provides his main suspects with a watertight alibi. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Robert Stephens, Amanda Burton and Philip Middlemiss

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Penhale decides to have a Police Open day to raise community awareness, but the only genuine visitor is an angry farmer who has had three of her sheep stolen. Meanwhile, Morwenna decides to take Al away for the weekend to try out Hannah's new glamping site, but the trip does not go well as Al accidently burns down the yurt. Elsewhere, James has started biting not only Louisa but other children at the nursery. Louisa speaks to Tina about it, but is surprised to find out Tina has spoken to their lecturer about it too

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: It is the annual Portwenn versus Port Carran gig race and school gig festival, and this time Penhale is the umpire and Morwenna is the Portwenn team captain. However, Morwenna is forced to find a replacement rower when Eric dislocates his finger. Meanwhile, Mel Hendy goes to see Martin at the surgery worried about a rash that could be infectious, but she ends up opening up to him about her concerns over her husband's drinking. Elsewhere, Martin and Louisa arrive home to find Chris Parsons waiting for them

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An American businessman fears his life is in danger while travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris, and offers fellow passenger Poirot $10,000 for protection. After rejecting the proposal, the sleuth wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow in Serbia. Racing against time to find the killer among the passengers, Poirot must look to the past to solve the crime, and is faced with a decision that will haunt him for ever. David Suchet takes the title role in the murder mystery, with guest stars Hugh Bonneville, David Morrissey and Eileen Atkins

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ashley falls for Maxine and is thrilled when she asks him to model for her. Gail and Alma fall out over the business between Mike and Stephen

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike is worried sick because of Stephen's actions. Gail tries to sort out the aggro between herself and Alma. Ken has a terrible day signing Daniel over to Denise

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The day of Linda's funeral arrives. Jo does not understand why Alan has changed his mind about America. Jack tells Rachel he is willing to leave Sarah

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Billy tells Andy not to trust the Sugdens. Biff insists Kim was responsible for Linda's death. Terry feels let down by Alan and decides to work to rule

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Convicted murderer Henry Stoddard's release from prison coincides with a spate of vandalism, and locals presume he is responsible for the petty crime. But when the former jailbird insists he did not kill PC Machin all those years ago, Walker reopens the case and races to stop Machin's son exacting revenge. Meanwhile, Peggy sees the chance to make some money when her birth certificate suggests she is related to Lord Ashfordly

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike attempts to earn some cash without Sid's interference. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Angie persuades Robert to arrange an evening out with the lads _ but neither she nor Ida are prepared for the consequences

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An American tourist's body is discovered in her hotel room, apparently dead from a heart attack - but a valuable Anglo-Saxon jewel she intended to donate to the Ashmolean Museum is missing and several interested parties are acting strangely. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Simon Callow, Roberta Taylor and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Local boy Barry Dyson is accused of a series of arson attacks - and his clandestine relationship with a former employer's daughter complicates his plea of innocence. Peggy's latest money-making scheme with old friend Denzil creates an eyesore, forcing Jack to use his artistic flair to rectify matters, before he and Blaketon resort to more drastic - and illegal - measures. Rural drama, starring Gwen Taylor and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy tries to keep the peace between Sam and Butch. Paddy is shocked to see his credit card bill and worries that he will not be able to pay it if Zoe lets him go

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah is not happy when Jack stays out all night. Tara tells Kim that she plans to divorce Alex. Ned goes to Oakwell Hall and sets fire to the stables

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre tells Ken it is obvious that Sue fancies him. Gail tries to make up with Alma. Mike assures Sally she still has a job, but he will need to lay off half the workforce

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike finds a buyer for his flats, Martin rows with Alma for putting the cafe on the market, and Judy tells Joyce that she might be pregnant

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott enter the world of botany to search for the killer of a retired teacher found dead shortly after smuggling a rare orchid out of Borneo. The discovery of two more bodies makes the detectives realise the murderer will stop at nothing to get hold of the coveted plant, and it seems the key to the mystery may lie in a Latin notebook. John Nettles and John Hopkins star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An American tourist's body is discovered in her hotel room, apparently dead from a heart attack - but a valuable Anglo-Saxon jewel she intended to donate to the Ashmolean Museum is missing and several interested parties are acting strangely. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Simon Callow, Roberta Taylor and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Michael Kitchen returns to the role of Christopher Foyle, senior intelligence officer for MI5, in the first of three films. Foyle is drawn into the world of corrupt Nazi businessmen when a university professor is found murdered in a London park. When it transpires the victim was working as a translator in Nuremberg, the detective realises there are powerful people trying to conceal secrets from the Second World War. Meanwhile, Sam (Honeysuckle Weeks) volunteers for a risky undercover job, despite being pregnant. Guest stars include John Mahoney (Frasier), Jaime Winstone (Mad Dogs) and Nigel Lindsay (Four Lions)

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: When the Metropolitan Police discover the burglary after the long Easter weekend, the gang members already have a two-day lead on them. They are desperate to split up the stolen goods, but more delays frustrate them. However, they soon come to realise that it is not only the police who are chasing them - Brian Reader also wants his share

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: Harry is called on to protect a Russian author whom the Protectors smuggled into Britain and who is now publishing his anti-socialist book. Drama, starring Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar agrees to help a girl find her missing brother and becomes embroiled in a drug-smuggling operation. Roger Moore and Burt Kwouk star

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Conclusion. Robin and his outlaw band storm the evil Baron de Belleme's stronghold in a bid to rescue Lady Marion and retrieve the silver arrow. Michael Praed, Anthony Valentine and Judi Trott star

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed and Miss King are called into action when a modern-day Jack the Ripper targets members of an international disarmament conference. Patrick Macnee and Linda Thorson star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: The prospect of a police reward persuades Arthur to go on the trail of a petty criminal who has stolen a suitcase crammed with money. However, it transpires a gang of international counterfeiters is also after the cash. George Cole stars

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A crooked foreign diplomat helps arrange the theft of a consignment of silver bullion being transported through Tilbury docks. Bodie and Doyle are assigned to investigate, and it becomes apparent that they have a useful link to the villains via Doyle's girlfriend Deborah. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The Flying Squad foils the hijacking of a security truck before attempting to learn the identity of an informer. Colin Welland guest stars in the hard-hitting police drama, alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar finds a suspicious building in Wales which is being used as a research laboratory, plunging him into the midst of a spine-chilling adventure. Spy drama, directed by and starring Roger Moore

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Robin saves a witch's life and accepts Marion fully into the band of outlaws. Fantasy drama, starring Michael Praed

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: The world teeters on the brink of war as an ingenious mastermind manages to frame Miss King to look like a traitor as part of his plan to trigger a major conflict. Classic fantasy spy drama, starring Patrick Macnee and Linda Thorson

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry acts as the bodyguard for a Lebanese businessman and diplomat. However, relationships between the two are strained and when their safe house is raided by gunmen, Terry's opinion of his charge reaches a new depth. Dennis Waterman and Kenneth Griffith star

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie works alongside an old friend who once saved his life while serving in the SAS, and has difficulty coping with mounting evidence that his ex-colleague has joined the gang of Italian terrorists they are shadowing. Crime drama, starring Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: With Haskins in Toronto, his stand-in DCI Quirk (guest star Bill Maynard) becomes obsessed with catching a gang of thieves. When information is received suggesting the crooks are about to rob a jeweller's shop, officers are poised for action, but events take an unexpected turn as the informant seemingly has a score to settle with Quirk first. Hard-hitting police drama, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Cycling: La Vuelta a Espana Highlights 2020
programme.desc: Action from stage eight, which featured a mountainous 164.5km route from Logrono to Alto de Moncalvillo. Gary Imlach presents, with Daniel Friebe reporting and Ned Boulting and David Millar on commentary

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Emmerdale actor Tony Audenshaw (Bob Hope), former gymnast Beth Tweddle, 2010 Strictly Come Dancing champion Kara Tointon and X Factor judge Louis Walsh compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by taking on one of the show's resident trivia experts in a test of their general knowledge. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: First Among Equals: The Laurie Cunningham Story
programme.desc: The remarkable career of the footballer who became the first Englishman to transfer to Real Madrid and the first black player to represent England in a competitive match. Cunningham inspired a generation of black youngsters before his untimely death at the age of 33 in 1989. Featuring contributions by Ron Atkinson, John Barnes, John Fashanu, Paul Ince and Ian Wright among others

programme.desc: A reckless slacker joins the Navy, hoping to win the respect of his girlfriend's father, a stern admiral. Sci-fi adventure, starring Taylor Kitsch, Liam Neeson and Alexander Skarsgard

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar investigates the suspicious deaths of five company directors, who each received a card with the words 'The Scales of Justice' printed on it prior to their demise. However, he finds his inquiries frustrated by the remaining members of the board. Roger Moore stars

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: The outlaws are attacked by a group of angry Crusaders who believe Robin is a thief. Michael Praed stars

programme.title: Darts: European Championship Live
programme.desc: Jacqui Oatley presents all the action from day one of the annual tournament from Konig Pilsener Arena in Oberhausen, Germany, featuring the first-round matches, played over the best of 11 legs. England's 2018 world champion Rob Cross overcame a strong field to become the victorious player in this competition last year. He prevailed 6-3 against Darren Webster in his opener and went on to defeat Gerwyn Price 11-6 in the final. Also on day one, there were two major upsets, with four-time champion of this event and top seed Michael van Gerwen losing a thriller 6-5 against Ross Smith, and current world title-holder Peter Wright going out 6-4 against Jeffrey de Zwaan. With analysis from Chris Mason and Alan Warriner-Little, and commentary by Stuart Pyke and John Rawling

programme.title: Cycling: La Vuelta a Espana Highlights 2020
programme.desc: Action from stage nine, featuring a 163.6km route from Castrillo del Val to Aguilar de Campoo. Gary Imlach presents, with Daniel Friebe reporting and Ned Boulting and David Millar on commentary

programme.title: Car Crash Global: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Documentary featuring dash-cam video footage of a host of astonishing accidents, spectacular smashes and enthralling escapes from roads all over the world

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A young widow is swindled out of her husband's insurance money by con artist Freddie Fenton (Derek Jacobi), so Terry responds by holding the crook's beloved Rolls Royce hostage. Comedy drama, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: The entire precinct's festive holiday celebrations are cut short when officers are called to examine a ruthless homicide linked to Eddie Flores, the deranged nephew of a notable drug lord who has a previous history with Riggs. The season of goodwill is weighing heavily on Riggs however, who flashes back to past Christmases spent in the company of his late wife Miranda. Murtaugh is also a little preoccupied with his neighbour's obnoxious holiday decorations. Action drama based on the film series, starring Clayne Crawford and Damon Wayans

programme.title: Minder

programme.title: Autumn at Jimmy's Farm
programme.desc: Following life on Jimmy Doherty's farm during the autumn months, as the team prepare the farm and wildlife park for the incoming seasonal changes. This time, the wildlife park takes delivery of their brand-new insectivore, an anteater, and with mating season beginning, Nero the stud ram is introduced to his ewes. Elsewhere, Jimmy gets to grips with this autumn's piglets

programme.title: Damilola: The Boy Next Door
programme.desc: A poignant and reflective documentary marking the 20th anniversary of 10-year-old Damilola Taylor's violent murder on a North Peckham Estate in south London, presented by the radio presenter, writer and former resident of the estate Yinka Bokinni, who was a childhood friend of Damilola. Throughout the film, Yinka attempts to reconcile the two versions of her past and try to understand how the warm and loving community she remembers so fondly from her childhood was presented in the media as a notorious, crime-ridden estate that was somehow to blame for the tragic loss of a young life

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A look back at a host of classic dates from the show, where eyes met, sparks flew, and love truly blossomed. Dapper gent Will is set up on a date with fellow tweed admirer Fran, while Bristol Rastafarian and single mother Ibiba meets fellow Bristolian Aarron. Elsewhere, Octogenarian Doreen meets 86-year-old author James, who is more than ready to write the next chapter of his love life

programme.title: Random Acts
programme.desc: A collection of exciting new films from cutting-edge black filmmakers, this time featuring Rikki Henry's short film Venus/Mars

programme.title: Sing It Loud: Black and Proud
programme.desc: From the legendary Ronnie Scott's venue, Maya Jama presents a one-off celebration for Black History Month, with some of the UK's finest gifted young jazz musicians. Zara McFarlane, Ashley Henry, Poppy Ajudha, Reuben James and Ayanna Witter-Johnson all take to stage to perform their own interpretations of iconic black protest anthems from Nina Simone, Stevie Wonder, Billie Holiday, Marvin Gaye and James Brown

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Mo Gilligan and Babatunde Aleshe are among the famous faces hitting the couch, joining Britain's favourite opinionated viewers as part of this year's Stand Up to Cancer appeal. Also taking part are Line of Duty's Adrian Dunbar, Vicky McClure and Martin Compston, KSI and Craig David, David Mitchell and Victoria Coren Mitchell, and Roger Daltrey and John Bishop. Shows they are appraising include Simply Nigella, Five Guys a Week, The Andrew Marr Show interview with Michael Gove, Family Fortunes, Scream, The Million Pound Cube, Emily's Story: Stand Up to Cancer, and news coverage of Manchester mayor Andy Burnham's skirmish with the Government

programme.title: Adult Material

programme.title: Big Eyes (2014)
programme.desc: Aspiring painter Margaret Keane marries a controlling man who sells her artwork. As the paintings of large-eyed children grow tremendously popular, Margaret discovers he is claiming to have painted them himself, and she and her daughter are forced to live a lie for years - until she finally resolves to prove his deceit in court. Tim Burton's fact-based drama, starring Amy Adams, Christoph Waltz and Terence Stamp

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: Britain's service industry is battling for customers, but the public need to be convinced the big names are doing all they can to limit risk to exposure. Many hotel chains, transport companies, restaurants and pubs are making big promises of 'deep cleans' and ''enhanced hygiene practices', but with the resurgence of Covid-19, Morland Sanders puts them to test and asks just how safe is it when we go out. Government public health guidance says regular cleaning of surfaces plays a vital role in limiting the transmission of Covid-19 in public settings. In a national experiment, the programme tests the cleanliness of the surfaces people might encounter on a commute, or going for a drink, a meal or a mini staycation? It also examines emerging research concerning airborne transmission of Covid, as well as meeting people whose wellbeing is reliant on the hygiene standards of the service industry

programme.desc: The couple prepare to celebrate their first Christmas together, but Mike struggles to find the perfect gift for Molly. Comedy, starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: The couple go to the opera, but Mike fails to enjoy himself due to a cold. With her sweetheart bed-ridden, Molly decides to play nursemaid, but ends up coming into conflict with her patient's mother, who does not appear to appreciate having her role as carer usurped

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Part two of three. Diane joins the political campaign standing against Sam's latest lover, who responds by trying to get her fired. Bar-room comedy, starring Ted Danson and Shelley Long

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Part three of three. Sam and Diane's loyalties are put to the test as polling day arrives. Starring Ted Danson and George Wendt

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray becomes the butt of embarrassing jibes for failing to consider Debra's feelings in a social crisis, and vows to be more attentive in future. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray gets the chance to cover the Super Bowl for his newspaper - but feels guilty about not asking Debra to accompany him. Ray Romano stars

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is horrified to learn that his mother used to read the diary he kept as a teenager, which contained his most personal thoughts. Ray Romano stars

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part two of two. The last-ever episode of the sitcom sees Daphne give birth to a son, Martin and Ronee tie the knot and Roz surprise besotted Noel with a kiss. Faced with an empty apartment, Frasier records his final radio show before jetting off to pastures new. Anthony LaPaglia and Richard E Grant guest star

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The psychiatrist abandons his bar-propping in Boston in favour of presenting a radio show in Seattle. However, his new life is thrown into upheaval when his father moves in with him - followed by apparently psychic care assistant Daphne. First-ever episode of the comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Tim Betts, the CEO of Muscle Maker Grill, goes undercover in the company he recently bought. Tim wants to make sure the brand is healthy and continues to expand, and he believes it's key that as they grow, each restaurant branch follows the same set of corporate standards. Tim thinks it's the perfect time to learn what's working and what needs an overhaul, so he meets staff including franchisees, a delivery driver, a food truck manager and a cashier whose flirtatious manner shocks him

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Includes headlines and weather

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef visits beachside restaurant Barefoot Bob's in Hull, Massachusetts, owned by husband-and-wife team Marc and Lisa. Every year profits nosedive through the bleak winter months, and matters aren't improved when it becomes clear they have both lost their passion - not only for the business but also for each other. With the restaurant and marriage on the line, Gordon tries to help turn things around before it's too late

programme.title: Steph's Packed Lunch
programme.desc: Steph McGovern presents the weekday magazine, including celebrity guests, food ideas, real-life features and entertainment, lifestyle and consumer stories. Plus, a sideways look at the news in One O'Clock Views

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Nick Hewer and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game in which contestants pit their wits against the clock. Colin Murray is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman and Laura Hamilton help a couple to choose between a property on the western coastline of Wales and a home in Carcassonne in the south of France

programme.title: The Great House Giveaway
programme.desc: Strangers Harry and Kaylum buy a majestic detached house in Ashton-under-Lyne in Greater Manchester at auction - then have six months to renovate and sell it on for a profit. Can they put their differences aside and turn this property around?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet up for a final showdown, as Kaye and Lily bring Harriet and Richard's blind issues to light before dishing the dirt on Sophie and David's discoveries. Amanda wants to know why frank feelings were unleased when it came to her canine companion, Trudy. David and Sophie banish Harriet and Richard's bathroom blues, before confronting Kaye and Lily with their cleanliness critique. After opinions are aired and differences debated, the payments are revealed and the winner is announced

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: t's the final night in Edinburgh, hosted by food and fashion-conscious Bruce. All week Bruce has expressed his desire for the finer things in life and his dislike of anything that falls below his high standards, so expectations are high. Can Bruce pull the evening off with style, and will he do enough to grab the cash?

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge has plastic surgery in an effort to lose weight, but a mix-up results in her being given breast implants instead - much to Homer's delight. With the guest voices of Adam West and Burt Ward, stars of 1960s TV series Batman

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart and his family enter the twilight zone in three more cartoon tales of horror, including the discovery of Bart's demented twin in the attic and Lisa being the god to an alternative human species cultured in a margarine tub. Plus, Homer tries to expose two fraudulent presidential candidates

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off: An Extra Slice
programme.desc: Jo Brand is joined by Jonathan Ross, Darren Harriott and Siobhan McSweeney to discuss all the action from a Bake Off first - Japanese Week - featuring an interview with the eliminated contestant. Tom Allen puts a select group of home bakers through their paces from a safe distance, and Jo showcases more of the weird and wonderful delights from viewers' kitchens

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions and the lively discussions that are prompted by the week's biggest and best shows

programme.title: The Last Leg
programme.desc: Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker are joined by celebrity guests for a comic review of the significant moments of the past seven days. As usual, the hashtag isitok paves the way for the gang to round up, examine and explain the leading and most entertaining news stories of the week

programme.title: Random Acts
programme.desc: Black film-maker Denise Alder brings West African art alive in Mablo Micasso's Dream, a surreal and beautiful journey through culture and myth. This is showing as part of Channel 4's programming for Black History Month, which offers an important platform for different aspects of the Black experience in modern Britain

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A look back at a host of classic dates from the show, where eyes met, sparks flew, and love truly blossomed. Dapper gent Will is set up on a date with fellow tweed admirer Fran, while Bristol Rastafarian and single mother Ibiba meets fellow Bristolian Aarron. Elsewhere, Octogenarian Doreen meets 86-year-old author James, who is more than ready to write the next chapter of his love life

programme.title: The Witch (2015)
programme.desc: An English farmer is driven from his land in 17th-century America, and settles on the edge of a sinister forest. The family suffers a series of inexplicable disasters, and as tension rises between them, the eldest daughter finds herself accused by her own parents and siblings of being a witch. Period horror, starring Anya Taylor-Joy and Ralph Ineson

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Promotions representative Dawn Griffiths hosts the first dinner party in Middlesbrough, and has confidence in her Asian-themed evening of curry and traditional Indian dancing. However, her rowdy guests - practical joker Ron Gordon, market trader Kay Griffiths, teacher Karena Serdecka and refuse collector Paul Tidy - prove hard to please

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Practical joker Ron Gordon hosts the second party in Middlesbrough, but despite his expertly cooked food, his hopes of an evening of elegance are placed in jeopardy by market trader Kay Griffiths, who dominated proceedings the previous night, but irritated her fellow diners

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Self-confessed loudmouth Kay Griffiths hosts the third dinner party in Middlesbrough, hoping to liven up her guests with good food, colourful conversation, a little conflict and her home-made punch

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Drama teacher Karena Serdecka hosts the fourth dinner party in Middlesbrough, treating her guests to a Dutch and Polish menu, before taking to the stage to sing with her band

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Refuse collector Paul Tidy hosts the final evening in Middlesbrough, throwing a film star-themed party in the hope of impressing his guests. His plans take a turn for the worse when one of his diners expresses a dislike of garlic, which happens to be one of the chef's favourite ingredients, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Food Unwrapped Investigates
programme.desc: Kate Quilton uncovers the truth behind some of Britain's most shocking food headlines, meeting the case studies behind the stories as well as exploring the science, conducting experiments and questioning those in control. She begins by investigating the world of severe food allergies, enlisting the help of a team of sufferers to report undercover at a host of different London eateries to see how they are treated

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris embarks on a thousand-mile trip along Spain's complex rail system, beginning in Gibraltar by exploring the vast network of military tunnels that have helped keep it under British control for more than 200 years. In Catalunya, he learns the role played by the railways in some of the bloodiest battles of the Spanish Civil War, then visits the spot where Hitler and Franco met during the Second World War

programme.title: Inside the Tower of London
programme.desc: The tower staff prepares to reopen to paying visitors after lockdown results in the longest closure in 75 years, beginning with a special ceremonial lowering of the drawbridge. The ravenmaster makes sure his birds are ready to interact with members of the public after months in isolation, while the chief curator oversees the launch of a gruesome new attraction commemorating the executions on Tower Hill

programme.title: Henry VIII: Rise of a Tyrant
programme.desc: The series concludes with a look at the king's final years, when the pain caused by an infected ulcer on his leg had a detrimental impact on his increasingly angry temperament. Lauren Johnson examines the king's exotic diet, including meat from porpoises and seals, and considers its impact on both his weight and his health. Plus, a look at a Catholic rebellion and the breakdown of Henry's relationship with advisor Thomas Cromwell

programme.title: Philpott: 5 Mistakes That Caught the Killer
programme.desc: The errors made by Mick Philpott that led to his arrest for causing the death of five of his children and their half-brother by setting light to his home in Derby. The programme examines how his unconvincing appearance at a press conference and ill-timed attendance of a karaoke party raised the police's suspicions - leading them to bug his hotel room and hear an incriminating conversation

programme.title: The Murder of Sally Anne Bowman: 15 Years On
programme.desc: Looking back at the high-profile case of Sally Anne Bowman's murder in 2005, the 18-year-old woman who was attacked on the doorstep of her home in London. The programme reexamines the eventual arrest of Mark Dixie, who was finally found guilty of the crime and given life imprisonment three years after her death in 2008

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes at Hull Royal Infirmary to show the night-time challenges facing NHS staff working in the A&E department. In the first edition, a 34-year-old man is brought in after having punched his arm through a pub window. Handcuffed and accompanied by four police officers, his behaviour has medical staff fearing for the wellbeing of other patients. Matron Kat Henry deals with a flirtatious patient who has cut his head after a night out on the town and is determined to leave the hospital without treatment

programme.title: NCIS

programme.desc: A look at how patients should decide whether to go to A&E or a GP, including the case of a patient who has recently completed a course of antibiotics for an insect bite on his face. programme.title: Critical Condition
programme.desc: A woman collapses unexpectedly during a day out with her family to an adventure park, and is left unable to move or walk. programme.title: Skin A&E

Plus, psoriasis-sufferer John feels he's exhausted all the options for treatment, but can Dr Emma McMullen offer him a ray of hope?

programme.title: Our Miracle Baby: Bringing Kia Home

programme.title: Sunny Bunnies

programme.title: Jeremy Vine
programme.desc: Lively magazine discussing the issues of the day, with co-host Storm Huntley and a mix of debate and viewers' phone-in calls

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Max and Steve try to recover almost £2,000 owed by the owner of a bridal shop in London, while officers Gary and Cona in Kent chase over £4,000 of vet fees after two neighbouring dogs got into a fight. In south west London, agents try to collect almost £11,500 owed by a small business

programme.title: 5 News at Lunchtime
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Carol and Trevor Gilmour hoped to renovate a barn and turn it into their dream home, but have been forced to live in a caravan during a long-running dispute with their neighbour over the property's front garden. Plus, a young couple gather shocking footage of the constant harassment they endured, and a farmer is suspected of killing a family's dogs

programme.title: Access

programme.title: Home and Away

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Dipi and Pierce have embarked on an affair, and their respective guilt makes them treat their partners incredibly generously, while Clive wants to talk things through with Sheila

programme.title: My Husband's Double Life (2018)
programme.desc: After a woman acts on her suspicions of her husband's infidelity, she discovers that not only does he have another wife but illegal business dealings with his father-in-law that he will stop at nothing to protect. It soon becomes clear her marriage is not the only thing on the line. Drama, starring Amy Nuttall, Daniel Lapaine and Dragan Micanovic

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel tries to decide what to do next, while Phoebe takes some of the heat off her by claiming she is the one who is pregnant. Chandler has trouble on the dance floor and Joey tries to impress an important Broadway producer with his acting ability. Jennifer Aniston and Lisa Kudrow star

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey becomes convinced Phoebe is pregnant and proposes to her so she will not have to be a single mother - but Monica quickly tells him the truth. Meanwhile, Chandler and Ross misplace the disposable cameras used during the wedding

programme.title: 5 News at 5
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Dipi and Pierce have embarked on an affair, and their respective guilt makes them treat their partners incredibly generously, while Clive wants to talk things through with Sheila

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: When Jai gets sick during Dean's first night caring for him, Ziggy has to step in. Angelo tries to get information about Dean from Alf and John

programme.title: 5 News Tonight
programme.desc: A round-up of the evening's headlines, including coverage of the latest national and international stories

programme.title: The Gadget Show
programme.desc: Craig Charles hosts a special edition of the show dedicated to AI, looking into how this technology could change life in the future. Ortis Deley is challenged to compose a new theme music for the show using nothing but artificial intelligence, and Georgie Barrat pays a visit to Ocado Technology to check out the latest advances in robotic technology. Plus, The Chase's Paul Sinha offers tips on the best AI assistants on the market

programme.title: Secret Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian explores the area between Glasgow and Edinburgh, paying a visit to the Royal Botanical Gardens and setting to work as an assistant keeper at Edinburgh Zoo. She learns about Scotland's forgotten ceramics industry during a visit to the seaside suburb of Portobello, gets a crash course in laying tables on the Royal Yacht Britannia, and gains special access to the country's busiest railway station

programme.title: Friday on the Farm
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Jules Hudson present a Halloween-themed episode, in which Rob and Dave take a trip to Warwickshire to stock up on pumpkins ahead of a carving contest in Barnsley. Peter Wright heads to Kent for seasonal hop picking, and JB Gill gets some tips on growing habanero chillies in the UK. A teenage shepherd provides some tips on sheepdog training

programme.title: Fifty Shades Freed (2018)
programme.desc: As tycoon Christian Grey and his new wife, Anastasia revel in a life of luxury, their happiness is endangered by her former boss. Conclusion of the romantic thriller trilogy based on EL James' novel, starring Dakota Johnson, Jamie Dornan and Eric Johnson

programme.title: Access
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: Fifty Shades Freed (2018)
programme.desc: As tycoon Christian Grey and his new wife, Anastasia revel in a life of luxury, their happiness is endangered by her former boss. Conclusion of the romantic thriller trilogy based on EL James' novel, starring Dakota Johnson, Jamie Dornan and Eric Johnson

programme.title: Good Girls' Guide to Kinky Sex
programme.desc: The couples continue to try new things in the bedroom - or rather not, as this week they are challenged to be more creative in their choice of love-making locations. They also use a spinning wheel game to discover new erogenous zones, getting to know each other's bodies in ways they have never done before

programme.title: The 21.co.uk Live Casino Show
programme.desc: Interactive gambling

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Bishop is more determined than ever to seek revenge for the murder of Qasim, her deceased boyfriend, after Gibbs and the team find a new lead in the Chen case. Meanwhile, Torres teaches the agents the art of pickpocketing

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee discovers his flat has been torn apart by a robber trying to locate an item of great value hidden somewhere by the convicted criminal who previously owned the unit. Meanwhile, congress woman Flemming tries to persuade Director Vance to pursue a career in politics. Mary Stuart Masterson guest stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: In a bid to carry out a sailor's dying wish, Gibbs and the NCIS team re-investigate a murder case that was originally ruled an accidental death. As the investigation proceeds, the case reminds Torres of a tragic time from his past

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: Engineer Rob Bell sets out on a journey to discover how six of Britain's most stunning bridges were designed and built. Completed in 1890, the Forth Rail Bridge now carries around 200 trains every day. The last of the great Victorian engineering triumphs, it has grown to symbolise Scotland as much as Edinburgh Castle, bagpipes or kilts. But, as Rob learns, this incredible engineering achievement was born from the tragedy of the 1879 Tay Bridge disaster, and 73 deaths were connected with its construction and immediate aftermath

programme.title: 7th Cavalry (1956)
programme.desc: An army officer is branded a coward for failing to fight in the Battle of the Little Bighorn, and is unable to convince anyone that official business had detained him. In a bid to repair his damaged reputation, he leads a cavalry detail to retrieve the dead from the battlefield. Western, starring Randolph Scott, Jay C Flippen and Jeanette Nolan

programme.title: The Court-Martial of Billy Mitchell (1955)
programme.desc: In the 1920s, an American colonel accuses the war department of criminal negligence for denying the air force the funding it needs - but the powers-that-be still regard aircraft as an irrelevant part of the military, and respond by putting him on trial. Courtroom drama, starring Gary Cooper and Rod Steiger, with Ralph Bellamy and Elizabeth Montgomery

programme.title: The Tin Star (1957)
programme.desc: A world-weary bounty hunter, formerly a sheriff, arrives in a small town where a newly-appointed lawman is struggling to keep the peace. When he learns of the mercenary's past, the inexperienced lawman seeks his help in learning the tricks of the trade so he can bring an outlaw gang to justice. Anthony Mann's Western, starring Henry Fonda, Anthony Perkins, Lee Van Cleef, Betsy Palmer and Michel Ray

programme.title: The Man Who Knew Too Much (1956)
programme.desc: An American couple on holiday in Morocco witness the killing of a local man they have befriended. Before dying he whispers a secret that has serious political implications - but they are prevented from telling the police when their son is kidnapped by a gang of spies bent on keeping them silent. Alfred Hitchcock's remake of his own 1934 thriller, with James Stewart, Doris Day, Bernard Miles and Daniel Gelin. Featuring Day's performance of Oscar-winning song Whatever Will Be, Will Be (Que Sera, Sera)

programme.title: Romancing the Stone (1984)
programme.desc: A romantic novelist goes in search of her kidnapped sister in the steamy jungles of South America, only to become the target of a villainous general who demands as a ransom the valuable treasure map in her possession. Help is at hand in the form of a craggy daredevil fortune hunter, who has his eye on a share of the profits. Comedy adventure, starring Michael Douglas, Kathleen Turner and Danny DeVito

programme.title: Jack Ryan: Shadow Recruit (2014)
programme.desc: A former soldier injured in the line of duty takes up a new career as a financial analyst for the CIA. However, his deskbound position changes to a dangerous undercover assignment when he discovers a plot to destroy the US economy. Spy thriller inspired by Tom Clancy's series of novels, starring Chris Pine, Keira Knightley, Kevin Costner and Kenneth Branagh, who also directed

programme.title: The Secret of Marrowbone (2017)
programme.desc: A young man and his three siblings have kept the death of their beloved mother secret in order to remain together. However, they are plagued by a sinister presence in their sprawling manor. Writer/director Sergio G Sanchez's horror, starring George MacKay, Anya Taylor-Joy and Charlie Heaton

programme.title: Mother! (2017)
programme.desc: A couple's relationship is tested when uninvited guests arrive at their home, disrupting their peaceful existence. Drama, starring Jennifer Lawrence, Javier Bardem, Ed Harris and Michelle Pfeiffer

programme.title: The Last Command (1955)
programme.desc: Adventurer Jim Bowie returns to Texas during the 1830s and tries to quell the simmering antagonism between settlers and the Mexican government. However, as the Mexican army's aggression intensifies, he is forced to take up arms and fight in the historic battle at the Alamo. Western, starring Sterling Hayden, Arthur Hunnicutt, Anna Maria Alberghetti and Richard Carlson

programme.title: The Last Blitzkrieg (1959)
programme.desc: The son of a Nazi general is selected for a secret undercover mission. He and his men disguise themselves as American soldiers and go behind the Allied lines, with the intention of carrying out acts of sabotage and undermining enemy morale. Second World War drama, starring Van Johnson and Kerwin Mathews

programme.title: Pursued (1947)
programme.desc: A brooding cowboy yearns to find peace, but his life seems to follow a tragic path because of the actions and hatred of a mysterious one-armed stranger and a forgotten incident from his childhood. Western, starring Robert Mitchum, Teresa Wright, Dean Jagger, Judith Anderson, Alan Hale and John Rodney

programme.title: The Gentle Sex (1943)
programme.desc: Seven women from vastly different backgrounds are brought together as volunteers in the Auxiliary Territorial Service during the Second World War. Despite their differences they become firm friends during training and on assignment driving military transport and operating anti-aircraft guns. Drama, starring Rosamund John, Joan Greenwood, Lilli Palmer, John Justin and Joan Gates

programme.title: The Huntsman: Winter's War (2016)
programme.desc: Eric and fellow warrior Sara, raised as members of ice Queen Freya's army, try to conceal their forbidden love as they fight to survive the wicked intentions of both Freya and her sister Ravenna. However, as war between the siblings escalates, Eric and Sara unite with Freya in an attempt to end Ravenna's wicked reign once and for all. Fantasy adventure, starring Chris Hemsworth, Charlize Theron, Emily Blunt and Jessica Chastain

programme.title: Sicario (2015)
programme.desc: An FBI agent is recruited to join an elite government task force based on the border between the US and Mexico, charged with preventing drugs being smuggled into the country. The team is sent on a secret mission, and the new recruit is forced to question everything she believes in order to survive. Crime drama, starring Emily Blunt, Josh Brolin and Benicio Del Toro

programme.title: Cold Skin (2017)
programme.desc: In 1914, a man named Friend takes a position as a meteorologist on a remote island. The only other inhabitant is a lighthouse keeper, who is reluctant to let his new companion live up to his name. But they are not alone. Fantasy horror, starring Ray Stevenson, David Oakes and Aura Garrido

programme.title: The Descent (2005)
programme.desc: A woman recovering from a personal tragedy joins a group of friends on a caving expedition. However, their adventure takes a horrific turn when they find themselves trapped in an unexplored cavern, where savage creatures are lurking in the shadows. Horror from Dog Soldiers director Neil Marshall, starring Shauna Macdonald, Natalie Mendoza, Alex Reid and Nora-Jane Noone

programme.title: The Last Frontier (1955)
programme.desc: A backwoods trapper signs up as a cavalry scout at a fort manned by inexperienced soldiers, where he falls in love with a colonel's wife. On taking over command of the garrison, the officer plans to lead his men in a reckless attack on a hostile Indian tribe - and it is down to the frontiersman to save his comrades from a massacre. Western, starring Victor Mature, Robert Preston and Anne Bancroft

programme.title: Millions Like Us (1943)
programme.desc: A woman is happy to be called up for service during the Second World War, but disappointed to learn she will be working in a factory making aircraft parts. However, the new job brings romance in the shape of a handsome airman and a close circle of friends in her co-workers, who all rally round when tragedy strikes. Drama, starring Patricia Roc, Eric Portman, Gordon Jackson and Basil Radford

programme.title: Rawhide (1951)
programme.desc: Outlaws hold staff and a passenger and her baby hostage at a remote stagecoach station while awaiting the arrival of a shipment of $100,000 in gold bullion. The nervous captives try to stay calm, but the tension between the gang leader and a jumpy henchman grows fraught, ending in a frantic shoot-out - with the infant in the thick of the fray. Western, starring Tyrone Power, Susan Hayward and Jack Elam

programme.title: The Thirty-Nine Steps (1978)
programme.desc: An innocent man is informed of a devious plan to start the First World War by murdering a foreign minister. Almost immediately, he is framed for murder and embarks on a perilous cross-country trek to prove his innocence, pursued by both the police and ruthless spies. Thriller - the third film adaptation of John Buchan's novel - starring Robert Powell, Karen Dotrice, John Mills and George Baker

programme.title: Eddie the Eagle (2016)
programme.desc: Biopic of British sporting underdog Eddie Edwards. Determined to fulfil his dreams of becoming an Olympic athlete, Edwards trains as a ski jumper - a field in which Britain had no other competitors. He secures a place in the 1988 Winter Olympics, where his can-do attitude makes him a media sensation in the face of a losing streak. Starring Taron Egerton, Hugh Jackman and Christopher Walken

programme.title: Independence Day: Resurgence (2016)
programme.desc: Two decades have passed since Earth was saved from an extraterrestrial invasion, and in the years since, the aliens' salvaged technology has been used to transform the world. However, it may not be enough to save the human race when a second, even deadlier attack begins. Sci-fi adventure, starring Jeff Goldblum, Liam Hemsworth and Bill Pullman

programme.title: Train to Busan (2016)
programme.desc: Hedge-fund manager Seok-woo sets off on a train journey with his small daughter, but as their train is about to leave the station a very sick passenger stumbles aboard - affected by a chemical spill that turns creatures and people into zombies. The virus spreads fast from carriage to carriage, and Seok-woo must join with other unaffected passengers and fight to save his own life and that of his child. South Korean horror, starring Yoo Gong and Yu-mi Jung. In Korean and Hawaiian

programme.title: Juan of the Dead (2011)
programme.desc: Cuba is overrun by brain-eating zombies, which the government tries to cover up as the work of American-backed dissidents. A 40-year-old slacker realises what is going on, and hatches a scheme to go into business as a zombie slayer, laying the dead to rest for a price. Comedy horror, starring Alexis Diaz de Villegas and Jorge Molina. In Spanish

programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester
