programme.title: Sabrina the Teenage Witch
programme.desc: Aunts Hilda and Zelda experience growing pains when they turn into teenagers for the night. S01 Ep15

programme.title: Sabrina the Teenage Witch
programme.desc: Sabrina takes a holiday to Mars with her aunts, where a handsome skiing coach sweeps her off her feet. Meanwhile, Salem seizes the opportunity to hold a party for the neighbourhood cats while the house is empty, and Hilda meets a man claiming to be a secret agent

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Ted sets up an architecture company in his apartment - but progress is hampered by the stationery order taking priority over his clients. When his intern starts sleeping with Robin, Barney becomes upset and confesses his feelings to Marshall, who takes it on himself to save the day. American sitcom, starring Josh Radnor, Alyson Hannigan and Neil Patrick Harris

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Ted's friends urge him to wait three days before calling a girl he likes to ask her on a date, but he insists on sending text messages - only to find, after some apparent initial success, that Barney is the person receiving them. American sitcom, starring Josh Radnor, Alyson Hannigan and Neil Patrick Harris

programme.title: Speechless
programme.desc: JJ gets into an argument with Kenneth over the amount of time they spend together, so Dylan decides she must 'parent-trap' them both so they can get along well again. When the family starts sneaking into a country club, Ray sees it as an opportunity to live the high life, but Maya jumps at the chance to spend more mother-and-son time

programme.title: Speechless
programme.desc: The DiMeos throw their annual Oscar party and invite some other families along to celebrate Hollywood's biggest night, but Maya feels threatened by new mum Becca

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj and Howard register Sheldon with an online dating site hoping to find his perfect female equivalent. Meanwhile, a drunk Penny seduces Leonard, but regrets it the next day. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco. Mayim Bialik (Blossom) makes her first appearance on the show

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny is stunned when Sheldon announces he is about to go on his first-ever date, and is even more surprised to find herself tagging along. Meanwhile, Leonard and Raj take a trip to the hospital after Howard's new robotic arm creates problems

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam and his friends are recruited to the William Penn Academy baseball team. Barry remains in denial about his pitching skills, while Geoff's father interferes in his relationship with Erica

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Assisted by Geoff, Erica and Valley Erica find a new band member to replace Lainey, but Barry convinces Geoff that the new musician is trying to steal Erica from him. Adam and Murray each plot to get out of going to Valentine's Day dinner with Beverly

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Part one of two. Peralta and Diaz adjust to life behind bars, but while Jake is forced to join a prison gang led by notorious inmate Romero, Rosa puts Captain Holt and Sgt Jeffords to the test by making outrageous lists of chores for her

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Part two of two. When the warden tells Peralta to investigate Romero's drug smuggling operation, Peralta finds himself trapped by divided loyalties. Meanwhile, Santiago is offered a deal by a local mobster who claims he can prove Lt Hawkins is guilty of the crimes that Diaz and Peralta were jailed for, but Holt and the team think they have found a safer plan. Tim Meadows, Lou Diamond Phillips and Gina Gershon guest star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Penny ask Howard and Bernadette for advice when they argue about money. Meanwhile, Sheldon and Raj try to find out whether they could survive a dark matter research expedition in a salt mine by attempting to simulate the conditions in a steam tunnel beneath the university. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny is left in a difficult professional situation when a doctor (guest star Billy Bob Thornton) turns up at her door following her flirtatious sales technique, so the guys try to help her out. Meanwhile, Amy and Bernadette have a dispute over whether female scientists should flaunt their sexuality to try to get ahead at work. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: Scrubs
programme.desc: Relationships between the Sacred Heart Hospital staff continue to suffer in the wake of Jordan's revelations, and Carla gives Turk the elbow, forcing him to stay with JD - who is desperately trying to get back into Dr Cox's good books. Guest starring Colin Hay, with Zach Braff, Donald Faison and Sarah Chalke

programme.title: Scrubs
programme.desc: JD, Turk and Elliot spend their first unsupervised night on the ward as resident physicians, a car chase ends at the hospital, and Dr Cox faces up to the feelings he has for his ex-wife. Comedy, starring Zach Braff, Donald Faison and Sarah Chalke

programme.title: Speechless
programme.desc: When some new students with disabilities start at Lafayette High School, Maya encourages their parents to demand full-time aides like Kenneth. However, the school administrators tell her that anything the new students get will be taken out of JJ's budget. Dylan starts to play truant because she is unused to returning to the same school for two years in a row, while JJ pushes Kenneth towards a romance with his teacher

programme.title: Speechless
programme.desc: Ray gets in over his head when he makes up a fake charity to impress his girlfriend, while Maya gives a newly promoted Jimmy tips on being the boss

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Gangster Seamus Murphy asks Holt to pay back the favour he owes for getting Diaz and Peralta out of jail, so the cops try to help the captain keep his end of the bargain without breaking the law

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: After mobster Seamus Murphy threatens Kevin, Holt devises an elaborate plan to hide his husband in a safe house, with Peralta as his guard. However as the squad members work overtime to find Seamus and take him down, Kevin and Peralta grow miserable with being cooped up alone together

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly insists the family take a road trip to go on vacation to Disneyland before Erica and Barry go off to college. Erica worries about her relationship with Geoff when he tells her they need to talk. Comedy, guest starring Christie Brinkley

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam is surprised when he bumps into his first love, Dana. Meanwhile, Erica and Barry find out they have nowhere to live on their college campus

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Penny agree to participate in an unusual experiment, and Leonard, Amy, Raj and Emily spend the evening trying to escape from a 'zombie'. Elsewhere, Howard and Bernadette run into trouble at the airport after returning from Mrs Wolowitz's funeral. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Trouble arises when Amy learns that Sheldon has applied for a one-way mission to colonise Mars. Meanwhile, Leonard surprises Penny with a gift, and Raj decides to snoop around Emily's flat after she is called back to work during their evening together. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Ren decides to host an Earth Day telethon, Barry vows to help her book some A-list talent. Beverly and her nemesis square off to find a perfect match for Bill

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies sets tasks for a group of comedians, with help from Alex Horne. In this edition, Jon Richardson strives to create the perfect music video

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Denise Van Outen and her partner Eddie, Mo Gilligan and Babatunde Aleshe, Nick Grimshaw and his niece Liv, Joe Swash and Stacey Solomon, Zoe Ball and her son Woody Cook, Martin Kemp and son Roman Kemp, Maureen Lipman and Gyles Brandreth, Johnny Vegas and his PA Bev, Shaun Ryder and Bez, Emilia Fox and Joanna David, Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford, Laura Whitmore and Iain Stirling, Rylan Clark-Neal and Linda Clark, Georgia Taylor and Sunetra Sarker, and Susanna Reid and Rob Rinder offer sharp, insightful, funny and sometimes emotional critiques. Shows under scrutiny include 127 Hours, Planet Earth II, Cape Fear, This Morning on public toilets, EastEnders, Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad, and BBC news reports on beaches and travel

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, focusing on programmes including After Life, Britain's Got Talent, The Andrew Marr Show, The Fantastical Factory of Curious Craft, Orphan, Harry's Heroes: Euro Having a Laugh and First Dates Hotel

programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer
programme.desc: The new principal forces Giles to organise the school's talent show, and he soon has a mystery on his hands when a dancer is found with her heart cut out. Buffy and her friends suspect the killer is one of the other acts, and a ventriloquist whose dummy seems a little too lifelike is the most likely culprit - but appearances are deceptive

programme.title: Angel
programme.desc: The sleuth consults a Hindu guru in an effort to end his obsession with Darla, and while he is away, Wesley assumes the mantle of the vampire with a soul

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard's fear of commitment causes him to avoid Bernadette, but he soon realises he has made a mistake and tries to compensate for it. Johnny Galecki, Simon Helberg and Kaley Cuoco star, with an appearance by Katee Sackhoff (Battlestar Galactica) as herself

Together with some help from Arthur, the Strawbridge boys have their work cut out as they create dishes they hope will strike a chord with everyone

However, when Kat is left to manage the project alone, the whole construction hangs in the balance

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Shannon Slater-Dent and Trevor Tredgett's Bove Town House in Glastonbury, but a poor night's sleep bothers Kathryn and Trevor from Treberthan B&B

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest continues at SugarBeat Eating House in Swainsthorpe, Norfolk, run by Stevie King and David Lovett, but the establishment's cleanliness quickly comes under fire

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Fritton Arms in Norfolk, run by Ben Davenport and partner Sian Dewaal. David and Stevie are upset about their feedback so they conduct a forensic-style room inspection - complete with rubber gloves - and a paddleboat race on a nearby lake brings out everyone's competitive nature. Ben impresses the guests by revealing the hotel's aristocratic connections - but soon comes under fire for allowing pets to stay

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners reunite to find out what they have been paid, with Shannon and Trevor from Bove Town House wanting to discuss Kathryn's comments about her poor night's sleep

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Black rhino Kitani is due to give birth at Chester Zoo and help swell the ranks of this endangered species, but, having lost three previous calves, it is a worrying time and the keepers are concerned that things aren't going to plan. The zoo also has four rare new arrivals called tenrecs, mysterious mammals from Madagascar and, with so little known about them, the keepers try everything they can to help them breed, while red river hog Mali has given birth to twins, but tearaway dad Confetti is a threat to his own babies

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau

After a sumptuous breakfast, the cars are on their way, and Dick, Angel, Arthur and Dorothy can focus on a retro vehicle of their own, the 1980s Asquith bus. With Angel's unique design, and Dick's engineering, it soon becomes the camper van the family dreamed of

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia

programme.desc: Henry Cole gives Simon O'Brien a motorcycling masterclass in Wiltshire as the duo seek more items to fix up and sell on to make profits for their owners

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree host their last wedding of the year, and they welcome a fleet of vintage Citroens, whose drivers are making a pit-stop on a charity tour around France. After a sumptuous breakfast, the cars are on their way, and Dick, Angel, Arthur and Dorothy can focus on a retro vehicle of their own, the 1980s Asquith bus. With Angel's unique design, and Dick's engineering, it soon becomes the camper van the family dreamed of

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton heads to Cornwall to help serial renovators Neil and Suzie with a contemporary build that is not just set to be very grand, but Suzie wants it finished by Christmas

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Richard III in Middleham, North Yorkshire, a pub run by Ken Ward and his wife Sharon, who become suspicious when one couple check out before breakfast. The village is known for its horse racing links, but one of the competitors, Matt Wilson, has a fear of horses, and another couple are concerned at staying above a noisy pub

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the Court House Unique Boutique B&B in Shelsley Walsh, Worcestershire, where hosts Keith and Lisa Shearin hope to impress. On arrival, Ken and Sharon are blown away by their honeymoon suite and the seemingly low price. Suspicions over price fixing drive Matt and Paul to the internet to verify the room rate, where they discover an anomaly, and despite Keith's best efforts at breakfast, Jo and Louis dismiss it as being too fancy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Castley Camp in Otley, West Yorkshire, for the third day of the contest, owned by single mum Jo Stevenson and her son Louis. Some of the guests are horrified by stains on their bedding and dust in their room, while an afternoon boot camp fitness session reveals Keith's competitive side. At feedback, Jo is left fuming over the comments about cleanliness and breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final two days see the contestants staying the night at the Lion & Swan in Congleton, Cheshire, run by owner Paul Whyle and manager Matt Wilson. A dirty bathroom and a waterbed cause upset, while some guests are still fuming about previous negative feedback. However, the worst is left for breakfast, when Sharon reveals she has discovered their rooms online at a higher price than they were told, upsetting Keith and Lisa and leaving the hosts determined to clear their names

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. First up are Ken and Sharon from the Richard III, who want to learn more about what's been causing Jo and Louis to lose sleep. Next, Keith and Lisa from the Court House Unique Boutique B&B want to challenge Matt about the allegations of price fixing and vehemently fight their corner. Needless to say, there is tension in the air and axes to grind, before the winner is revealed

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: The keepers try to work out why male red panda Jung is losing weight, with a full medical examination scheduled after a special diet fails to solve the problem. West African mandrill JC is being challenged for his dominant position by his sons Ludo and Jareth and, while JC is taken for some health checks, Ludo gets 'over-friendly' with his dad's girlfriend. Tempers start to fray among Chester Zoo's Humboldt penguin colony during the annual moult - can youngsters Frazzle and Munch avoid being picked on by the grown-ups?

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: As the beginning of autumn arrives, Dick Strawbridge passes on his conker skills to the family, before the serious business resumes and the next phase of the renovation begins. Dick and his wife Angel Adoree get to work converting the old tack room into a grand entrance way. Angel knocks down walls and upcycles an old glass door, while Dick lays a new concrete floor

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Peter Maddison meets a Brisbane resident with a passion for all things Japanese, who has hired one of the country's most pioneering architects to design his new home

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud returns to Sussex to see how Ben Law's woodsman's cottage has changed. The house was built on a low budget, using methods dating back to the early medieval period with raw materials from the surrounding countryside. During the past 18 months the upstairs has been completed and the outside has been landscaped. Kevin also discovers how the property has changed Ben's life since the original programme was broadcast

programme.title: Billion Pound Cruise
programme.desc: The Symphony of the Seas sails into port, where 6,680 guests get off and on the ship. As always, it will be a mammoth task for the crew members as they try to keep to the schedule

programme.title: When Cruises Go Wrong
programme.desc: In a typical tourist season, around two million British people will take to the seas for the holiday of a lifetime promising class, elegance and refinement. However, as this documentary reveals, it's not always plain sailing. Cameras capture the downside of cruising, from sickness and crime to terrifying weather, collisions and emergency evacuations

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Stephan, 39, is rushed to St George's with internal bleeding from his femoral artery, caused by complications following an angiogram he had the day before, and doctors are concerned his heart could be under potentially fatal strain. Nineteen-year-old Emmanuel Jr arrives in A&E after suffering an acute sickle cell crisis, and his mum Elizabeth talks about the impact the condition has had on the family, while 40-year-old Fernanda is admitted with abdominal pain, but the test results reveal a surprising reason for her discomfort

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Vic Reeves and Aisling Bea join host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson for more comic innuendos, unpredictable conundrums and unforgivable puns in the comedy quiz. David O'Doherty is with Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Billion Pound Cruise
programme.desc: The Symphony of the Seas sails into port, where 6,680 guests get off and on the ship. As always, it will be a mammoth task for the crew members as they try to keep to the schedule

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick fears the worst when a young cockapoo is hit by a lawnmower, and he faces the challenge of saving as much of its leg as possible. Meanwhile, a seven-year-old labrador struggles with pain in its elbows, and a German Shepherd-Collie cross is suffering with a bulging disk in his spin and hip dysplasia

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits one of Kenya's largest tea plantations and discovers that tea comes in multiple grades and flavours, and every batch can be subtly different. Matt Tebbutt dives in Cornwall for the edible seaweed that has started cropping up on supermarket shelves and Kate Quilton heads to Slovenia and Northern Ireland, eager to learn about the increasing use of rosemary in foodstuffs

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp visits Manhattan, where she creates candy canes and lollipops at artisan sugar crafter Papabubble, before heading back to the UK to learn how to make passion fruit marshmallows covered in chocolate and coconut

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A couple who met 10 years ago and now run a B&B together in Cornwall search for a property on the Greek island of Naxos, on a budget of £215,000. Presented by Scarlette Douglas

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit the Cotswolds and Somerset, where finds include a set of milk churns and an old munitions shell

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien discover items in Wensleydale, North Yorkshire, that are ready for upcycling and hopefully turn a profit for their owners

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: As the beginning of autumn arrives, Dick Strawbridge passes on his conker skills to the family, before the serious business resumes and the next phase of the renovation begins. Dick and his wife Angel Adoree get to work converting the old tack room into a grand entrance way. Angel knocks down walls and upcycles an old glass door, while Dick lays a new concrete floor

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton helps a couple who cannot decide what to do with their plot in south Wales, with the 1950s bungalow currently on the site proving particularly problematic

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition gets under way at the Oak Tree Cottage in Burley, Hampshire, where owner Jacqui Castle and her sidekick Jane Waters hope the homely approach will win the day. Guests Martin and Catherine Crosswaite are not encouraged by the shared facilities, while breakfast served at a communal table is not to the liking of Norman Butcher and Lorraine Watkins

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Bath's the Rising Sun Inn, where owners Lorraine Hawkins and Norman Butcher hope to bring their guests up to standard by having a dress code. Following their feedback on the last visit, Jacqui and Jane take an extra critical eye to the rooms, while Martin and Catherine show their competitive streak during a boules match

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the Busfeild Arms in East Morton for the third day of the contest, where hosts Martin and Catherine Croswaite pride themselves in giving great Yorkshire hospitality. Jacqui is overwhelmed by the luxury and extras in the rooms, which raises questions about how the owners make a profit. Meanwhile, Martin shocks the group by sharing his stunt motorbike riding past, and later, Patrick feels the pressure of being the final host

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stopover is at Patrick Duggan's Beatles-themed Epstein House in Liverpool, the former home of band manager Brian Epstein's father. Jacqui and Jane are unimpressed by unpleasant smells in the bedroom, while Martin and Catherine show they will do anything to win during a game of Fab Four charades. At dinner, Patrick shocks his guests when he reveals his idea of the ideal woman. Will it have an effect on their feedback?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the final time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Jacqui and Jane from the Oak Tree Cottage want to find out why Norman and Lorraine wouldn't return, while Martin and Catherine from the Busfeild Arms have some explaining to do when it comes to their profit margins. Accusations fly around the table as payments are revealed and the winner is announced

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: The keepers are concerned about a three-month-old Andean bear cub, worrying that its mother's overprotective behaviour may curb its development. The pygmy marmosets have new babies and mum Audrey has to carry her twins everywhere she goes to ensure their safety, while young elephant Nandita is desperate to make friends with babies Aayu and Indali, but her boisterous behaviour is proving too much for them - can she learn the maternal skills she needs to win them round and persuade them to go swimming with her?

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Before winter arrives, Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree take on their next project, transforming a 35-foot long, eight-ton river boat into luxury guest accommodation. While Angel completely overhauls the old nautical interior with bespoke fabrics and unique crafts, Dick has the small task of installing the boat's very own wind turbine and a decking that reaches over the moat

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Peter Maddison meets Ronnen Goren and Trace Streeter, who plan to build an agricultural oasis in a 100m-long shed that gives new meaning to the concept of indoor-outdoor living

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Tim and Zoe Bawtree, who live in a costly Regency property in Cheltenham, Gloucestershire, plan to build a modern, low-maintenance house in their back garden. However, height restrictions mean 60 per cent of their new home has to be underground and there are 90 objections from neighbours to their planning application. Undeterred, the pair press ahead, only to encounter further problems - including the departure of the builders

programme.title: The Same Sky
programme.desc: Lars proves to be a natural at his Romeo assignment and effectively gets under Lauren's skin, leaving her prey to emotions that she thought she had buried. In German and English

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Tulsi is airlifted to St George's after falling from a cliff near her home in Hastings, and CT scans reveal she requires emergency brain surgery. Meanwhile, 39-year-old Adam is rushed to A&E after his motorbike collided with a car, leaving him with an obvious and painful leg injury, and 70-year-old retired mechanic Ron is treated after trapping his hand between two vehicles while helping his friend fix their car

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 22-year-old cyclist is rushed to St George's Hospital after he collided with a car on the way to work and 'bullseyed' its rear window. Since he was not wearing a helmet, doctors send him for a CT scan to assess whether he has sustained a serious head injury, or life-changing damage to his neck, spine or internal organs. Meanwhile, 91-year-old Peter arrives at A&E three nights after suffering a fall at home, and 26-year-old Emma needs treatment for a head wound sustained during an epileptic seizure

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Angler's Lodge in Island Park, Idaho. Owner Dave Eby built the lodge with his own hands, but a family tragedy has caused him and wife Dede to shy away from the hotel and the community. Gordon is desperate to help mend this broken family - but is it too late to save their business?

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Tulsi is airlifted to St George's after falling from a cliff near her home in Hastings, and CT scans reveal she requires emergency brain surgery. Meanwhile, 39-year-old Adam is rushed to A&E after his motorbike collided with a car, leaving him with an obvious and painful leg injury, and 70-year-old retired mechanic Ron is treated after trapping his hand between two vehicles while helping his friend fix their car

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Labrador that is both a guide for the blind and a seizure alert dog, and has become lame in both its front legs. Invasive surgery could mean that the dog's owner, Toni, is without her lifeline. Meanwhile, a couple hope that surgery will save their Rhodesian Ridgeback's leg from a cancerous tumour, and Fitzpatrick Referrals receptionist Natalie's Tibetan terrier becomes unwell

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton heads to Australia to investigate eucalyptus, which is found in throat lozenges and chewing gum, with a local farmer shows her the clever way the highly toxic plant is made safe for human consumption. Jimmy Doherty is in Poland to find out about sauerkraut, before learning about a remarkable new use for its juice in a French power station, while Matt Tebbutt examines whether coffee, tomatoes, potatoes or even red wine should be kept in the fridge

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike makes a mistake in Indiana, and while the guys get a warm reception from a carnie collector in Ohio, the pick brings an unexpected sting

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys visit a man who has not thrown away anything since the 1950s, and is not willing to start now. And he also has a penchant for swimming trunks

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson subjects the Skoda Yeti to a thorough examination, while Richard Hammond charts the evolution of the Porsche 911 by pitting a modern Turbo S cabriolet against its oldest relative the Volkswagen Beetle - and the power of gravity. James May takes the Ariel Atom V8 for a spin on the test track, and comedian John Bishop receives tuition from the Stig before getting behind the wheel of the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters square up to their Australian counterparts in an automotive version of the Ashes, with challenges including rallying, drifting and a double-decker car race. Jeremy Clarkson also test-drives the Ferrari 599 GTO, and Boris Becker gets behind the wheel of the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May set out to boost the British economy by embarking on a trade mission to India. Equipped with three old British cars and a range of uniquely British products, they set off on an epic road trip across one of the world's most fascinating and challenging countries, devotedly promoting Britain - with distinctly mixed results

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond travel to Africa, where they are challenged to buy a second-hand car costing less than £1,500 and in no way designed to go off-road, and drive across Botswana. Along the way they must negotiate salt pans, rivers and reserves full of animals while facing sweltering heat and suffocating dust

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May head to Norway to find out how a selection of cars fare when taking part in Winter Olympic events. Their experiments include firing a rocket-powered Mini off a ski jump, tackling the biathlon with 4x4s, staging a game of motorised ice hockey and timing the Jaguar XK in a speed-skating race

programme.title: Big Zuu's Big Eats
programme.desc: Big Zuu is going regal as he heads to Royal Leamington Spa to meet and eat with comedian and actor Guz Khan. Leamington was once a place Queen Victoria visited, now it's the mandems turn

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade takes actress and writer Jessica Hynes on a whirlwind weekend away to Iceland, a country famous for spectacular water features, geo-thermic pools and a curious belief in elves - all attributes that the pair investigate with ruthless efficiency. They go on the popular Golden Circle Tour of the Nordic island's most famous waterfalls, glaciers and geysers - by helicopter, saving seven valuable hours. They also eat rotten shark and Brenavin, a spirit made from mashed potato, and attend elf school, followed by a speedy boat trip in search of whales. There's also a rapid chill-out at the Blue Lagoon geothermic pool and spa

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Charles Dance, Stephen Mangan, Isy Suttie and Gok Wan join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the comedy panel show, trying to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Rob Brydon hosts

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are sent to Bolivia, where they are challenged to travel through the rainforest and journey to the coast of Chile, driving second-hand off-road vehicles bought locally for a maximum of £3,500 each. Along the way, they encounter drug lords and feel the effects of altitude sickness as they negotiate the region's roads, one of which is a notoriously dangerous 40-mile mountain pass dubbed El Camino de la Muerte - the Death Road

programme.title: Top Gear: From A-Z
programme.desc: John Bishop narrates an alphabetical romp through the series' most memorable stunts and features as former hosts Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May pushed vehicles of all shapes and sizes to their limits. With 22 seasons of the show, there was no shortage of material to choose from as the likes of Tom Cruise and Cameron Diaz attempted to be the fastest Star in a Reasonably Priced Car, and the presenters risked life and limb in their assorted globe-trotting adventures

programme.title: Big Zuu's Big Eats
programme.desc: Big Zuu is back on home turf - cooking for comedian Phil Wang, who is performing his Philly Philly Wang Wang show in London's West End.

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by comedy actor Stephen Mangan for a weekend in the Moroccan city. Wanting to experience its famous street food, the pair sample a local delicacy of steamed sheep's head - an eye-opening culinary experience for them both. They then take a hot-air balloon flight over the Atlas Mountains with breathtaking views, watch a traditional story-telling performance and visit the historic Saadian tombs, as well as Marrakech's ancient tanneries. Finally, in search of a suitable place to sum up the holiday experience, Richard and Stephen head into the desert for a contemplative camel ride

programme.desc: Comedians Al Murray and Nish Kumar, presenter Anneka Rice and Radio 1 DJ Clara Amfo continue the daily test of their general knowledge skills, hosted by Richard Osman

programme.title: Top Gear: From A-Z
programme.desc: John Bishop narrates an alphabetical romp through the series' most memorable stunts and features as former hosts Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May pushed vehicles of all shapes and sizes to their limits. With 22 seasons of the show, there was no shortage of material to choose from as the likes of Tom Cruise and Cameron Diaz attempted to be the fastest Star in a Reasonably Priced Car, and the presenters risked life and limb in their assorted globe-trotting adventures

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond compete in a land race from Resolute, Canada, to the North Pole in their most ambitious challenge to date. Pitting horse power against dog power they travel 450 miles across mountainous land masses and jagged sea ice at temperatures as low as minus 65C

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on a road trip, trying to follow the path of the Three Wise Men across the Middle East. However, they opt to do so in open-top sports cars that were built for significantly less demanding tasks than driving along the conflict-ridden mountains of southern Turkey, through war-ravaged Iraq and across the searing deserts of Syria. The trio also try to find out what myrrh is and where it can be bought

programme.title: Big Zuu's Big Eats
programme.desc: Big Zuu and the boys are back on the road heading to Cardiff to cook for American stand-up and actor Desiree Burch, who is in the Welsh capital with her new show

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade explores the Austrian capital in the company of his IT Crowd co-star Chris O'Dowd. Over a packed mini-break, they sample the city's finest sausages, cakes, wines and spirits and visit fairgrounds, cafes and sewers, while Richard suffers an allergic reaction and Chris breaks something valuable

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nish Kumar, Clara Amfo, Anneka Rice and Al Murray compete in Double Points Friday, and their scores are tallied across the week resulting in an overall champion being crowned

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition. Question master Stephen Fry continues the comedy quiz's exploration of subjects beginning with the letter L as he asks a range of fiendish questions on the multisized worlds of the little and the large. Joining regular panellist Alan Davies are Pointless co-host Richard Osman and stand-ups Lucy Porter and Phill Jupitus, who are all hoping they don't fall into the QI elves' traps and set off the klaxon

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition. Question master Stephen Fry continues the comedy quiz's exploration of subjects beginning with the letter L as he asks a range of fiendish questions on the theme of long-lost things. Joining regular panellist Alan Davies are Madness frontman Suggs, Australian comedienne Claudia O'Doherty and stand-up Jimmy Carr, who are all hoping they don't fall into the QI elves' traps and set off the klaxon

programme.title: The Da Vinci Code (2006)
programme.desc: A respected academic goes on the run when he is framed for the murder of a French historian. Pursued by the detective on the case and a sinister assassin monk, he strives to unmask the culprit, leading to a conspiracy determined to preserve a 2,000-year-old secret at any cost. Thriller based on Dan Brown's novel, starring Tom Hanks, Audrey Tautou, Ian McKellen, Paul Bettany and Jean Reno

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and regulars Hugh Dennis and Andy Parsons are joined by Josh Widdicombe, Gary Delaney, Tiffany Stevenson and Romesh Ranganathan for the comedy quiz. The panellists give their take on major news stories from July 2014 and participate in a series of stand-up spots and improvised games

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Host Dara O Briain and regulars Hugh Dennis and Andy Parsons are joined by Ed Byrne, Milton Jones, Russell Kane and Zoe Lyons on the comedy quiz. The panellists give their take on the major news stories from July 2014 and participate in a series of stand-up spots and improvised games

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Broadcaster David Dimbleby takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz from April 2019, with investigative journalist Stacey Dooley and comedian Henning Wehn joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy play hot-tub fantasy date with Anna and Toby - and soon find themselves landing in hot water. One-time Spice Girl Emma Bunton and Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent appear as themselves, alongside regulars Lee Mack, Sally Bretton, Hugh Dennis and Abigail Cruttenden

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee faces a parenting dilemma as he worries that son Charlie is getting a reputation at school for being the class clown. But is it a simple case of the old adage 'like father, like son'? Sally Bretton co-stars, alongside Hugh Dennis and Selina Griffiths

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In

Over a packed mini-break, they sample the city's finest sausages, cakes, wines and spirits and visit fairgrounds, cafes and sewers, while Richard suffers an allergic reaction and Chris breaks something valuable

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Paul Hollywood delves into his family history, finding out about his maternal grandfather's experiences in the Second World War, serving as an anti-aircraft gunner in Tunisia and Italy. The Great British Bake Off judge then digs deeper, learning about his Scottish ancestry - and how one of his forebears held down a very demanding job

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: British-born actress Jane Seymour knows there is Jewish ancestry on her father's side of the family, but beyond that she knows very little, other than her own suspicions that they suffered in the Holocaust. As she researches her paternal great-aunts Jadwiga and Michaela, she soon finds out more about their experiences under Nazi occupation. The search takes her to Warsaw and France, uncovering stories of life in a ghetto and a terrifying flight to Switzerland from the Germans

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates the truth behind the legend of King Arthur, and looks for evidence of whether the mythical monarch was English, Scottish or Welsh

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: As the Red Army approached a forest in Poland toward the end of the war, they stumbled upon a massive complex containing more than a thousand structures across 25sqkm

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second of two episodes from Bolsover Castle in Derbyshire. Items brought in to be valued include a painting by DH Lawrence, a broken pot with a connection to the abolition of slavery, a 19th-century pistol, a collection of vintage hats, a complete set of Famous Five books and a rare jade pendant dug up in the garden of a 17th-century house

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A towering structure with an explosive past, a deserted cityscape, a mysterious top-secret facility and a vast Nazi construction

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the first leg of a journey from the coalfields of South Wales to the southernmost tip of Cornwall, travelling from Whitland, Carmarthenshire, to Swansea

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Pontyclun to Ebbw Vale in Wales. Along the way, he visits the Royal Mint in Llantrisant and Cardiff Arms Park, scene of the first Welsh victory over the All Blacks back in 1905. In Cardiff Bay, Michael discovers the departure point for one of the Edwardian era's most famous expeditions - Scott's ill-fated voyage to the Antarctic

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second programme this year from RAF Coningsby in Lincolnshire, and discovers the story of a length of tree that ended up inside a Lancaster bomber. Treasures brought in to be valued include a harp that survived the French Revolution, a curious wooden item with an unexpected purpose, a diamond brooch found hidden inside a barometer and a pair of Second World War propaganda posters printed on hessian sacking

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis channels his love of cars to transform a vintage Bentley engine part into a must-have light. He also turns an old icebox into a shining contemporary cabinet

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction of Airlander 10, the world's largest aircraft which combines elements of planes, helicopters and airships and is able to stay airborne for five days

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A devastated cliff-top ruin, an extraordinarily tall tower, a concrete structure stranded at sea and a heavy metal monster in a field all feature

programme.title: Holocaust Escape Tunnel
programme.desc: Archaeologists search for a rumoured escape route from a Nazi execution site in Poland that led to a nearby Synagogue, which was later destroyed by the Soviet army

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Did the ancients possess the knowledge and ability to create, and cut seemingly complex crystal skulls or do they have extra-terrestrial origins?

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville changes his image with the help of some trendy clothes and a medallion made from a cocoa tin lid, while Arkwright decides to increase his profits through advertising

programme.title: Open All Hours

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actor and stage director Derek Jacobi enjoyed a relatively humble upbringing in Leytonstone, London, where his father ran a sweet shop and his mother worked in a drapery, but research into his maternal side of the family soon uncovers a link to a far more intriguing past. Discovering that his great-grandmother's name was Salome Laplain, Jacobi finds that he is descended from a wealthy French Huguenot who fled religious persecution, and who has connections to royalty on this side of the channel

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Jerry Hall sets out to discover how her father's family, who she knew were originally from Oldham, ended up in the United States, and her investigation takes her from the cotton mills of Lancashire to the plains of Texas at the time of the railroad. She also explores her mother's side of the family and discovers her ancestors' pioneering roots as they headed west across the United States at the time of the Frontier, and finds out about a connection to the original pioneer and American folk hero, Daniel Boone

programme.title: Mission: Kill Hitler
programme.desc: A plot to kill the fuhrer that remained secret until the late 1990s. Operation Foxley planned to murder Hitler during his daily morning walk at the Berghof, his Bavarian residence. Two soldiers parachuted into the area were supposed to find a perfect location to shoot Hitler and stop the devastating war. Based on the British Secret Services' declassified documents, using archives and fictionalised reconstitutions, this film recounts the full story and the detailed plan that should have led to the murder of Adolf Hitler, and explains why it failed.

programme.title: Inside Hitler's Killing Machine
programme.desc: Documentary about the Holocaust and some of the key figures involved, beginning with Hjalmar Schacht, who was never a Nazi but played a vital role in Hitler's plans

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: The Day They Dropped the Bomb
programme.desc: Documentary marking the 70th anniversary of the day the world's first atomic bomb was detonated above the Japanese city of Hiroshima on August 6, 1945. Featuring a minute-by-minute account of what happened on that tragic day, through archive footage and interviews with eyewitnesses who rarely speak about their experience of the bombing. Also interviewed is navigator Theodore Van Kirk, who was the last surviving member of the crew who dropped the bomb, before his death in 2014

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Primary school teacher Laura Ford hosts the second dinner in Bristol, and prides herself on throwing a highly organised party, meticulously planning every detail of the event. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Stonemason Andy Wright hosts the final dinner party in Bristol, confident the black-tie theme and his impressive Victorian flat will win him the £1,000 prize. programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away

Rising to the challenge, with £385,000 to play with, Jonnie has three great properties to show them in the UK, and for her £150,000 budget Laura has three options up her sleeve in Corfu, but will the couple love any of them enough to put in an offer?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton and Jasmine Harman help Bob and Judith choose between the rugged coastline of northern Norfolk and the sunny beaches of the Costa Blanca on a budget of £230,000

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Matt Lucas host the show's first ever Japanese week. programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Alex Horne sets more tricky, puzzling and downright idiotic challenges for the celebrities, before Greg Davies decides who deserves the most points. programme.title: Married at First Sight

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Newcomers GB Para Powerlifter Ross and wellness coach Susie must make a dramatic decision about which of their teammates loses their place in camp. Sitting alongside the elegant Victorian and Edwardian properties in south-west London, this three-up, three-down is ugly from the outside and even worse on the inside, with dark, dated and badly designed spaces. Architects Katerina Dionysopoulou and Billy Mavropoulos are brought in to make most radical transformation on the programme to date - but will their wildly ambitious poured-concrete extension prove too innovative for the owners?

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George Clarke gets to work on a 300-year-old house in Broadstairs, which was once a school and a retirement community, leaving it with a few quirky features - not least a row of three toilets. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Andy Daykin hosts the first dinner party in Edinburgh, opting for a Scottish-themed night complete with haggis, bagpipes and whisky. His guests are self-described cougar Liz Grant, perfectionist Irena Magdolowic, political researcher Toni Giugliano, and rock chick Michelle Lindsay

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Irena Magdolowic hosts the second round of the culinary contest in Edinburgh, and believes her experience as a catering manager will provide her with the necessary skills to claim the £1,000 prize. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: PA and rock chick Michelle Lindsay hosts the penultimate dinner party in Edinburgh and is confident her bizarre array of dishes will impress her guests. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.desc: Rod and Margaret from London search for a house in Dorset and a holiday home in Dordogne in France, with enough space for their six grandchildren to come and visit

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George Clarke gets to work on a 300-year-old house in Broadstairs, which was once a school and a retirement community, leaving it with a few quirky features - not least a row of three toilets. For inspiration, he visits the Royal Pavilion in Brighton. He also helps a couple make the most of the tiny living room in their 1930s south London bungalow

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Matt Lucas host the show's first ever Japanese week. For the signature challenge the bakers tackle a Japanese version of the Asian staple, steamed buns, while the technical round involves a tricky recipe involving multiple layers. The showstopper round is themed around kawaii - the Japanese love of all things cute

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Harry Hill take on Jon Richardson and Rose Matafeo, while Alex Horne and the Horne Section join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr with Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions and the lively discussions that are prompted by the week's biggest and best shows

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Mo Gilligan and Babatunde Aleshe are among the famous faces hitting the couch for a special episode for this year's Stand Up to Cancer appeal. From Saturday night entertainment juggernauts to the week's biggest news stories, from hard-hitting documentary series to gritty drama, the contributors offers sharp, insightful, funny, and sometimes emotional critiques of popular and topical TV shows

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off: An Extra Slice

programme.title: Adult Material

programme.title: Guy Martin's Best Bits

He has entered it in the Nevada Open Road Challenge, tested it to its limits on Bonneville Salt Flats, and broken a world record at the Nurburgring in Germany

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In south London, father-and-son team Del and Dael Anglin face death threats from a debtor's father, after arriving at a property to collect a debt of over £3,500. programme.desc: A harmless tramp is attacked by an over-zealous vigilante and left for dead in a deserted alley, sending Briscoe and Logan in pursuit of his assailant. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Christopher Noth

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: In a bid to carry out a sailor's dying wish, Gibbs and the NCIS team re-investigate a murder case that was originally ruled an accidental death. As the investigation proceeds, the case reminds Torres of a tragic time from his past

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: When a US Marine is murdered at an event for the Honor Flight Network, a non-profit that arranges for veterans to visit the Second World War, Korea and Vietnam War Memorials for free, the NCIS team members have to rely on irritable Vietnam War veteran Henry Rogers, who seems to be the key to finding the culprit

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: After a vice admiral's laptop is infected with ransomware, he enlists McGee and the NCIS team to track down the hacker before the computer virus eliminates his personal and professional files. Bruce Boxleitner guest stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Torres is forced to rely on old case notes and contacts after a US Marine's murder is linked to a former dirt bike gang he once joined for a year-long undercover sting operation. Wilmer Valderrama stars

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: An assistant district attorney takes the law into his own hands by gunning down a hired killer following a series of death threats. The finger of suspicion immediately points to his wife, but when cracks appear in the seemingly airtight case, Goren and Eames are led on a different trail. Spin-off drama, starring Vincent D'Onofrio

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: When the assistant of a female videogame developer is attacked at a convention the team goes undercover online and discovers a world of misogynistic abuse by gamers. The detectives think they have found the perpetrators of the attack - but another attack is planned against the victim's employer

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team tries to stop a sex-trafficking ring after a mother goes undercover to find her daughter, who has been taken by the gang, and later Benson uncovers a shocking truth. Hard-hitting and emotional drama, starring Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The drama about an NYPD team specialising in sexual crimes returns. Benson tries to juggle work and her new role as a foster mother to Noah Porter. Meanwhile, a new detective assigned to the unit gets off to a rocky start. Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek star

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The SVU intervenes in a million-dollar clothing deal when a basketball superstar is accused of rape - but the team members disagree on how to handle the case. Hard-hitting and emotional series chronicling the life and crimes of the New York Police Department, starring Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo is reunited with his old flame, Jeanne Benoit, when insurgents in Sudan attack a group of volunteer doctors, including her husband. Travelling to the crime scene to conduct a joint murder-kidnapping investigation, Dinozzi and McGee offer their assistance in searching for the missing doctors. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Sean Murray, with Scottie Thompson

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Petty officer Kyle Friedgen is accused of killing his ex-girlfriend, but while his case is dismissed due to a mishandled warrant, his life and reputation are marred by suspicion that he got away with murder. Determined to clear his name in full, Friedgen volunteers to be court-martialed, on the condition that the NCIS team conducts its own investigation. Meanwhile, Bishop and Jake discuss their marital problems. Naval crime drama, starring Emily Wickersham, with Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis reveals how CCTV and technological advances are helping to ensure convictions, focusing on criminals in London involved in robberies, thefts, drug crimes, street brawls and weapons offences. In Soho, police officers monitor the activity of the area's 100,000 nightly visitors from a CCTV control room in an underground bunker, while in Lambeth the air support unit is called in when an armed gang are seen rampaging through the streets

Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin

programme.desc: A high-school student is found dead in an apartment building, and Briscoe and Green suspect an improper website may have played a part in her murder. As McCoy and Southerlyn investigate the case further, they realise the victim's best friend, who was reluctant to come forward as a witness, may provide the answers. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach, Jesse L Martin and Elisabeth Rohm

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A famous rock star is acquitted of raping a fan after the victim's claims are rejected. Logan and Briscoe decide to investigate further, and discover the musician's pleasant reputation does not quite match his personality. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Detectives Briscoe and Logan face a race against time to catch a serial killer carrying out a series of racially motivated murders. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Christopher Noth

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.desc: Carisi's future brother-in-law accuses his female parole officer of rape, forcing the detective to investigate whether his sister's fiance is telling the truth or allowing his old habits to resurface. Drama, starring Peter Scanavino and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A disturbed young man with a vendetta against women seeks revenge for being snubbed and bullied in his past, which leaves Rollins in danger as she tries to stop an attack. Elsewhere, Benson tries to find the time to see her son in hospital

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

Detective drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin

The hunt for her killer leads the team into a web of deceit and blackmail, with a Russian mobster and his son seemingly pulling the strings

programme.desc: Prince Charles is among those seeking to restore George III to his proper place in history, as diaries, letters and expert analysis paint a picture of a devoted family man, whose 60-year reign culminated in his receiving accolades as 'the father of the people', while scientists explode the myths surrounding his illness

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: A 47-year-old woman in denial about the poor state of her health becomes emotional when Shaw Somers reveals her cholesterol levels are dangerously high, and a 24-year-old seeks help in controlling her irritable bowel syndrome. Giovanni Miletto lends his expertise to a sports enthusiast who suffers from crippling premenstrual stress, and joins Lucy Jones to investigate whether a man's extreme raw food diet might be damaging his body

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Michael Mosley and the team are in Lancaster to find out how much air pollution a typical family breathes in and whether trees could be the answer to the problem. programme.desc: Thirty delegations from around the world arrive to stay at the hotel for the duration of the Olympics, with the red carpet being rolled out five times a day for heads of state. programme.title: Eat to Live Forever with Giles Coren
programme.desc: The food critic takes up three extreme regimens in an effort to push the limits of life expectancy and meets people from around the world whose pursuit of longevity is an obsession that dominates every aspect of their lives. He undertakes the calorie restriction diet, before going on one that consists only of foods hunted, fished or gathered by mankind's Paleolithic ancestors and ends with another made up almost entirely of fruit. Throughout the process, Giles's health is monitored by his doctor, who assesses the impact these dietary habits are having on his body

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: One-time soap stars take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. The teams are Dean Gaffney and John Altman (EastEnders), Ken Morley and Steven Arnold (Coronation Street), Dean Sullivan and Paul Usher (Brookside), and Darren Jeffries and Matt Littler (Hollyoaks). There is also an appearance by former X Factor contestant Johnny Robinson. Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: TV presenter Dan Lobb, musical theatre star Jodie Prenger, wildlife expert Chris Packham and Coronation Street actress Jennie McAlpine (Fiz Stape) compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by pitting their general knowledge against one of the show's resident trivia experts. programme.title: NFL: America's Game
programme.desc: The story of Green Bay Packers' 2010 Super Bowl success over Pittsburgh Steelers at the Cowboys Stadium in Arlington, Texas. This was the Packers' fourth overall win in the NFL's showpiece encounter, and their second since Super Bowl II under celebrated head coach Vince Lombardi, after whom the Super Bowl trophy is now named

programme.title: NFL: America's Game
programme.desc: A look back at Denver Broncos' 1997 Super Bowl triumph over Green Bay Packers at the Qualcomm Stadium in San Diego, California. This was the first victory in five attempts for the Broncos, and they were led to victory by Hall of Fame quarterback John Elway, who spent his entire career with the Denver side
